En: The lieutenant clears his throat. "Detective -- aren't you forgetting something?"
Zh: 警督清了清喉咙。“警探——你是不是忘了什么？”

En: Her journal? We should probably leaf through it. Might learn something useful.
Zh: “你指的是她的日记？我们应该翻阅一遍。也许能找到什么有用的东西。”

En: We still need to investigate the scene. Remember?
Zh: “我们还要调查现场。还记得吗？”

En: Of course. I was just testing you, to see if *you* remembered.
Zh: “当然了。我这是在考你呢，看看*你*记不记得。”

En: Aw, can't you do it Kim? I'm wiped out.
Zh: “噢，就不能你来吗，金？我已经没力气了。”

En: Sorry! (Hit your head.) "I forgot."
Zh: “抱歉！”（敲敲自己的脑袋。）“我忘了。”

En: The lieutenant only shakes his head. "Let's just do it, shall we?"
Zh: 警督只是摇了摇头。“我们赶紧动手吧，好吗？”

En: We still have to deal with her... He adjusts his coat. "Not pleasant, I know. But we can't leave her like that. And we have to investigate the scene, too."
Zh: “我们还要处理她的遗体……”他摆弄了一下大衣。“我知道，这不是什么愉快的活计，不过我们不能就这样放着她不管，而且我们还有现场要调查呢。”

En: But it's so sad, Kim. Too sad for me!
Zh: “但是实在是太悲伤了，金。我太悲伤了！”

En: I'd rather go outside and have a beer right now.
Zh: “我宁愿现在就出去喝杯啤酒。”

En: Yeah, you're right. Let's get it over with.
Zh: “你说得对，咱们赶紧把这事儿了了吧。”

En: Be that as it may, it doesn't change the fact. We have a duty to her. Let's see it through. He gestures back toward the tunnels. "After you."
Zh: “就算如此也无法改变这个事实：我们对她是有责任的。干活吧。”他朝着后面的隧道做了个手势。“你先走。”

En: He gestures back toward the tunnels. "After you."
Zh: 他朝着后面的隧道比了个手势。“你先走。”

En: Sorry! (Hit your head.) "I forgot."
Zh: “抱歉！”（敲敲自己的脑袋。）“我忘了。”

En: We still have to deal with her... He adjusts his coat. "Not pleasant, I know. But we can't leave her like that. And we have to investigate the scene, too."
Zh: “我们还要处理她的遗体……”他摆弄了一下大衣。“我知道，这不是什么愉快的活计，不过我们不能就这样放着她不管，而且我们还有现场要调查呢。”

En: Be that as it may, it doesn't change the fact. We have a duty to her. Let's see it through. He gestures back toward the tunnels. "After you."
Zh: “就算如此也无法改变这个事实：我们对她是有责任的。干活吧。”他朝着后面的隧道做了个手势。“你先走。”

En: Aw, can't you do it Kim? I'm wiped out.
Zh: “呃，不能你来吗，金？我已经没力气了。”

En: The lieutenant only shakes his head. "Let's just do it, shall we?"
Zh: 警督只是摇摇头。“我们赶紧动手吧，好吗？”

En: But it's so sad, Kim. Too sad for me!
Zh: “但是实在是太悲伤了，金。我太悲伤了！”

En: Her journal? We should probably leaf through it. Might learn something useful.
Zh: “你指的是她的日记？我们应该翻阅一遍。也许能找到什么有用的东西。”

En: We still need to investigate the scene. Remember?
Zh: “我们还要调查现场。还记得吗？”

En: Of course. I was just testing you, to see if *you* remembered.
Zh: “当然了。我这是在考你呢，看看*你*记不记得。”

En: I'd rather go outside and have a beer right now.
Zh: “我宁愿现在就出去喝杯啤酒。”

En: Yeah, you're right. Let's get it over with.
Zh: “你说得对，咱们赶紧把这事儿了了吧。”

En: The lieutenant clears his throat. "Detective -- aren't you forgetting something?"
Zh: 警督清了清喉咙。“警探——你是不是忘了什么？”

En: He gestures back toward the tunnels. "After you."
Zh: 他朝着后面的隧道比了个手势。“你先走。”

En: Behind the pipe -- the maintenance door, rusted shut and half-sunken in sand.
Zh: 管道后面——藏着扇维修门，紧紧地锈死了，有一半还掩埋在沙子里。

En: This isn't gonna work without the lieutenant here. What if you *do* find a way in and it's a *trap*? Kitsuragi needs to be here, covering you.
Zh: 不能没有警督在。你要是*真的*找到一条进去的路但却是个*陷阱*呢？必须有曷城在场为你提供掩护。

En: This isn't gonna work without the lieutenant here. What if you *do* find a way in and it's a *trap*? Kitsuragi needs to be here, covering you.
Zh: 不能没有警督在。你要是*真的*找到一条进去的路但却是个*陷阱*呢？必须有曷城在场为你提供掩护。

En: It's jammed shut. The subtle approach isn't going to work on this one.
Zh: 门卡得这么死，手再巧也没用。

En: Oh yeah... *big boy time*. This needs you to put your BACK into it.
Zh: 噢耶……现在是*大男孩时间*。你要全力以赴了。

En: It looks too exhausting to even try. You'd only bloody your fingers on the rust.
Zh: 试都不用试，光看着就非得累掉半条命不可，你的手指会被铁锈划破的。

En: I see. Another stop on your crusade against narcomania. I *was* hoping we were in pursuit of a murder suspect...
Zh: “我明白了。这是你对抗麻醉剂瘾的又一个站点。我*还*希望我们是在追查凶杀案的嫌疑人……”

En: Okay. What *else*?
Zh: 好吧。*还有*呢？

En: An old pipe peeks out from beneath the rotting boards of the boardwalk. Could this be an alternative path into the Feld building?
Zh: 一条老旧的管道从步道底下的烂木板下伸了出来。这条管道也能通入菲尔德大厦内部吗？

En: A building like this must have multiple doors serving various functions. Perhaps a basement access?
Zh: 像这种建筑肯定有很多扇门，每一扇都有不同的功能。也许这是地下室的入口？

En: The lieutenant looks over your shoulder.
Zh: 警督的越过你的肩膀朝里看。

En: What's in there?
Zh: 里面有什么？

En: [Leave.]
Zh: [离开。]

En: Try to pull the door apart.
Zh: 试着把门拉开。

En: A double door for a team of two.
Zh: 一面双扇门，专门为二人组准备的。

En: One More Door.
Zh: 又是一扇门。

En: Broke into Cuno's apt.
Zh: 破门闯入坤诺的公寓。

En: Broke into communist apt.
Zh: 破门闯入康米主义者的公寓。

En: Broke bookstore door.
Zh: 破门闯入书店。

En: Cursed Iilmaraan die.
Zh: 伊尔玛诅咒骰子。

En: Could we get into the Feld building through this pipe?
Zh: “我们可以通过管道进入菲尔德大厦吗？”

En: Could I get into the Feld building through this pipe?
Zh: 我能通过管道进入菲尔德大厦吗？

En: Given that this isn't a martial arts thriller, a grin flickers across his face, "it's highly unlikely -- and not without risk to our health either."
Zh: “鉴于这不是一部动作惊悚片，”他的脸上闪过一丝微笑，“这是不太可能的——而且也并非全无生命危险。”

En: Come closer and look in.
Zh: 靠近一些，往里面看。

En: Your eyes slowly begin to adjust to the darkness inside the drainage pipe.
Zh: 你的眼睛开始慢慢适应排水管里的黑暗。

En: [Leave.]
Zh: [离开。]

En: At first, you can't make anything out in the darkness at all.
Zh: 最开始，你在黑暗中根本什么也看不见。

En: The pipe is sleek with rain.
Zh: 管道因为雨水变得光滑。

En: A thin layer of snow gleams inside the pipe.
Zh: 一层薄薄的雪在管道里闪闪发光。

En: An ordinary drainage pipe. Darkness.
Zh: 一个普通的排水管。漆黑一片。

En: However, the pipe suggests there may be an entrance to the basement around... The lieutenant pushes aside the reeds and looks around.
Zh: “不过，这个管道意味着这周围可能真有一个通往地下室的入口……”警督拨开芦苇，环顾四周。

En: The lieutenant's face lights up. He doesn't usually notice these little things.
Zh: 警督的脸突然亮了起来。他通常不会注意到这些小东西。

En: This looks like a great place to stash narcotics. I want to make sure there's nothing hidden in there that children might pick up.
Zh: “这里看起来像是个藏匿麻药的好地方。我要确保里面没藏着什么小朋友不该捡到的东西。”

En: As your eyes adjust, you see some trash -- crumpled up newspapers, cigarette butts. Someone has half-heartedly spray-painted 'SKULLS' on the right side.
Zh: 等到双眼适应之后，你看见一些垃圾——皱巴巴的报纸，还有香烟头。有人在右边敷衍了事地喷涂了个‘骷髅头’。

En: You can't seem to pry the doors apart for long enough to slip through. A second pair of hands would be really useful here.
Zh: 你可以把门硬拉开的时间似乎不足以让你从门缝里钻进去。要是再来一双手就好了。

En: You and the lieutenant lean all your weight into pushing the doors apart, but you're not quite synchronized in your efforts. They slam shut again before you can enter.
Zh: 你和警督用尽全身的力量想把门拽开，但两人发力的时机却不一致。你们还没来得及挤进去，刚刚拉开的门就又关上了。

En: The lieutenant leans back against the door, panting. "Let's take a breather and try this again later? It's... very tough."
Zh: 警督靠在门上，气喘吁吁。“我们先喘口气，之后再试一次？还真是……挺牢固的。”

En: Good work. The lieutenant nods toward the impenetrable darkness inside. "Shall we?"
Zh: “做得好。”警督朝着眼前黑漆漆的洞口点了点头。“我们进去吧？”

En: [Go in.]
Zh: [进去。]

En: And? And nothing. Broken glass from bottles thrown against the walls of the pipe. A syringe...
Zh: 还有呢？也没什么特别的。扔到管壁上被砸碎的玻璃瓶碎屑。一支注射器……

En: And?
Zh: 还有呢？

En: A very, very faint smell of stale liquor still lingers -- or is it just your imagination?
Zh: 一股非常、非常微弱的陈年老酒味道仍然挥散不去——或者这只是你的想象？

En: He means: the pipe must be coming from *somewhere* in the building.
Zh: 他的意思是：管道也许是从大厦的*某个地方*延伸出来的。

En: Oh, duh, why didn't you think of that?
Zh: 哦，你怎么就没想到呢？

En: ...and it's right here! A maintenance door. He points to a rusted metal double door to the right of the pipe, obscured by the reeds.
Zh: “……在这儿！维修门。”他指向管道右侧一扇生锈的金属门，被芦苇给遮挡住了。

En: The metal doors are heavy and the flaking rust hurts your palms, but, together with the lieutenant, you manage to slide them open just enough to squeeze in.
Zh: 金属门很沉，上面剥落的铁锈把你的掌心弄的生疼，不过在警督的帮助下，你还是把门缝拽开到刚好够一个人溜进去的宽度。

En: The metal doors are heavy, and very awkward to open on your own, but you just manage to slide them open wide enough for you to slide yourself between them...
Zh: 金属门很沉，凭一己之力很难打开，但你还是想办法把门缝拽开到一个刚好够自己钻过去的宽度……

En: It's *much* easier from this side -- the panels on the rails slide once you dust the sand off. With the help of your partner from the other side the doors open with a *creak*.
Zh: 这样就*容易多了*——只要把沙子拂去，就会发现这两块金属板就安在滑轮轨道上。有了你的搭档在另一侧帮手，这扇门*嘎吱*一声打开了。

En: He's quite proud of himself.
Zh: 他对自己的表现颇为满意。

En: Yes. Time to investigate these passages. Let's move quietly and not make more sound.
Zh: “是的。是时候该调查一下通道了。我们悄悄地进去，别出声。”

En: Not *running* is a great way to do that.
Zh: 只要别*跑步*就能达成这个目标。

En: Maintenance entrance? So pedestrian.
Zh: “维修入口？真没劲。”

En: Yes, yes, it's *cool* to risk your life climbing rickety ladders. Anyway, let's investigate these passages...
Zh: “是啊是啊，要论*酷*的话怎么能跟冒着生命危险爬摇摇晃晃的梯子相媲美啊。总之，我们还是先调查一下这些通道吧……”

En: Two rusty metal plates that slide apart form a crude door. It's been here under the boardwalk for a while.
Zh: 两块生锈的金属板组成了一道简陋的大门。它在这个木板步道下面已经有一阵子了。

En: Who's there?
Zh: “谁在里面？”

En: Push the doors open.
Zh: 把门推开。

En: How did you even get there?
Zh: “你到底是怎么进来的？”

En: After you *climbed* up to the roof, you mean? There's a maintenance entrance under the boardwalk. It's next to a drain pipe, possibly a storm drain -- it was easy to miss before.
Zh: “你是说，在你*爬上*屋顶之后吗？木板步道下面有一个维修入口，就挨着排水管，也许是雨水沟——不是很好发现。”

En: You ready?
Zh: “你准备好了吗？”

En: Ignore the door. [Leave.]
Zh: 无视这扇门。[离开。]

En: Officer? Are you okay in there?
Zh: “警官？你在里面还好吗？”

En: What do you mean 'who's there'? It's me, Kim. Stop playing around and help me get this door open.
Zh: “什么*谁在里面*，你说什么呢？我是金啊，别闹了，快帮我把这扇门打开。”

En: The doors seem to be on rails, but they've gotten jammed. You grab a side and put some strength into prying it open. With the help of your partner the two metal panels slide open with a *creak*.
Zh: 这两扇门好像是有滑轮轨道的，不过已经卡住了。你抓住一侧，用力想把门强行拽开，你的搭档在另一侧帮手。伴随着一阵*嘎吱*声，两块金属板打开了。

En: You really ought to open this door. It's dangerous to go ahead alone.
Zh: 你应该把这扇门弄开，独自前行太危险了。

En: Huh, I hope no one *dangerous* heard that.
Zh: “哈，希望可别被什么*危险*的人听到才好。”

En: Could have saved me the effort.
Zh: “本来可以帮我省点麻烦的。”

En: At least now we know a quick way in and out.
Zh: “至少咱们找到了条捷径了。”

En: He shrugs. "At least now we have an exit. So, let's get going. It's time to investigate these passages..."
Zh: 他耸耸肩。“至少现在我们有个紧急出口了。好了，继续前进吧。是时候该调查一下这些通道了……”

En: Yes, we have an exit. So, let's get going. Time to investigate these passages... He peers into the darkness.
Zh: “没错，我们有紧急出口了。好了，继续前进吧。是时候调查一下这些通道了……”他凝视着眼前的黑暗。

En: We should've factored this in. Damn it.
Zh: 我们当初应该把这一点考虑进去的。见鬼。

En: So this is what she was scared to tell Titus. *This cop, this cop...*
Zh: 所以这就是她不敢告诉提图斯的事。*这个警察，这个警察……*

En: That strange distant fear is getting close now. It's a fear -- of *yourself.*
Zh: 现在那种遥远又诡异的恐惧感离你越来越近了。你在恐惧的——是*你自己*。

En: A fear fills you. A bad kind of fear. Of *yourself*.
Zh: 你全身都被恐惧占据了。那种很糟糕的恐惧。对*你自己*的恐惧。

En: Did someone mention a fucked up tie? I call BULLSHIT. You're too CRAZY to be corrupt.
Zh: 刚才有人提到一条操蛋的领带了吗？要我说的话，这都是放屁。你都疯成这样了，怎么可能会收人贿赂呢。

En: She's not going to change her mind that easily. She still perceives you as a threat.
Zh: 她不会这么轻易就改变主意的。她还觉得你是个威胁。

En: *No* idea who these people are. Literally.
Zh: *完全不知道*这些人是谁，真的。

En: Satellite-Officer Vicquemare looks out of the window grimly, then puts his coffee down and turns to Patrol Officer Minot. "We can either take a room here in the Whirling or go home for today."
Zh: 外派警官维克玛冷酷地看着窗外，然后把咖啡放下，转向巡警迈诺特。“我们要么在飞旋旅社开个房间，要么就今天直接回家。”

En: Let's go home, Jean. Nothing's going to happen today, she responds quietly. Jean takes his blond wig off. "Call Heidelstam. He can give us a ride."
Zh: “我们还是回去吧，让。今天不会再发生什么了，”她安静地回应道。让摘下了他的金色假发。“打给海德斯塔姆吧。他可以载我们一程。”

En: Keep calm. Breathe in. After the pain recedes it's a little clearer...
Zh: 保持冷静。深呼吸。疼痛减轻之后，神志清晰些了……

En: He couldn't take it like you. It irks him -- then he gets over it.
Zh: 他没法像你一样接受这件事，他为此而心焦——然后才慢慢缓过来。

En: I would have done the same... He has to catch his breath. "Had I not been incapacitated..."
Zh: “我也会这么做的……”他喘了口气。“如果我没有失去行动能力的话……”

En: Our job. It went really bad. And now we need to clean it up.
Zh: “这是我们的工作。现在情况不太妙。咱们必须把这里清理干净。”

En: He squints into the darkness, "that tent there."
Zh: 他望向黑暗深处，“那边的帐篷。”

En: She likes telling you about the machine. Keep her talking, look for an opportunity to break loose.
Zh: 她喜欢跟你聊机器的事。让她接着说下去，寻找脱身的机会。

En: Klaasje didn't want to turn the lamp on. She was afraid of another shot.
Zh: 卡拉洁不想开灯，她害怕凶手再来一枪。

En: Her grip on the gun is tight, her arms toned -- her posture solid. Martinaise lost a valuable defender.
Zh: 她持枪的手握得很紧，双臂紧绷——她的姿势稳如磐石。马丁内斯错失了一位宝贵的守护者。

En: Klaasje knew exactly what she was doing. You can't remain calm under pressure otherwise.
Zh: 卡拉洁很清楚自己在干嘛，不如此的话你很难保持冷静。

En: She lied to you... about that *too*.
Zh: 她对你撒谎了……*包括*那件事。

En: She doesn't believe you.
Zh: 她不相信你。

En: The darkness takes over as you feel yourself slipping into an unknown, mute state. Oh... it's death! This is the end.
Zh: 黑暗接管了一切，你感觉自己坠入到一个未知的、无声的世界。哦……这就是死亡！这就是结束。

En: Don't cover them.
Zh: 别盖上它们。

En: That's an awful decision. Why would you not want to shield yourself from it? It's an entirely *new* type of experience -- way worse than all the previous ones.
Zh: 这是个糟糕的决定。你怎么就不防着它点儿呢？你面对的是一种*全新*的体验——其可怕程度远超你之前所经历过的一切。

En: You should probably check on Kim. It doesn't sound like he's doing all too well.
Zh: 你也许应该看看金的状况。他现在的状态好像不太好。

En: Just keep her talking and you'll get through this.
Zh: 让她接着说，你能渡过难关。

En: Well, it doesn't *feel* much better, but you can form sentences now. Thinking doesn't seem to hurt as much.
Zh: 呃，虽然*感官上*并没有好太多，但你现在可以组织起语言了，思考起来也没那么痛了。

En: There's only three metres between you and the machine. If you keep her distracted for long enough, maybe...
Zh: 你现在离那台机器只有三米远。只要能让她继续分心下去，也许……

En: Be careful when you make your move -- that'll be *it* for questions. Bide your time.
Zh: 一定要谨慎决定下一步行动的时机——一旦开始下一步你就*没法*问她问题了。慎之，慎之。

En: How did you know we were coming?
Zh: “你怎么知道我们来了？”

En: I heard you in the passages. And I've been preparing for quite a while.
Zh: “你们在走廊里的时候我就听见了，而且在这之前我已经准备了好些时日了。”

En: By hiding bullets under floorboards?
Zh: “所谓的准备指的是把子弹藏在地板底下吗？”

En: So you found my shack, huh? I'm not surprised.
Zh: “这么说你是找到我的小屋了，是吗？对此我并不意外。”

En: Her tone is bitter. She thinks she's been betrayed.
Zh: 她的语气有些苦涩。她觉得自己被人出卖了。

En: She didn't rat you out, by the way -- Isobel, the washerwoman.
Zh: “对了，她没有出卖你——伊泽贝尔，那个洗衣女工。”

En: She didn't rat you out, by the way -- the washerwoman.
Zh: “对了，她没有出卖你——那个洗衣女工。”

En:
Preparing for a... shoot-out? That why you're hiding ammo?	“准备什么……枪战吗？所以把子弹先藏起来？”
0x0100001600009D44	Dialogue Text	LAIR / INSTIGATOR	Ruby, the Instigator	So nice." She smiles a little smile. "That's one knife I didn't want to find in my back."
Zh: “很好。”她微微笑了笑。“我可不想连她都在我背后捅我一刀。”

En: As opposed to the other knives she's finding there now. Hardie, for one.
Zh: 不像其他背刺她的人。比如说，哈迪兄弟。

En: Okay... then I have other questions...
Zh: “好吧……那我还有别的问题……”

En: Preparing for the... worst?
Zh: “准备什么……最坏的情况？”

En: You're... desperate, aren't you?
Zh: “你已经准备好……拼个鱼死网破了，是吗？”

En: You don't need that...
Zh: “你不必那样的……”

En: I was. Before I caught you in the pale latitude compressor. I'm fine now.
Zh: “前一秒的确是，但我现在已经用灰域维度压缩机控制住你们了，所以现在我没事了。”

En: Not anymore, no. This could've turned out pretty bad for me if you hadn't walked right into 25 bands of ultra-high frequencies.
Zh: “现在情势已经峰回路转了。如果你们没有一头撞进25超高频率波段的话，情况确实会对我极为不利。”

En: That's her admitting the bullet was an emergency exit.
Zh: 她的言外之意是那颗子弹是她留给自己的最后救赎。

En: It was dark in the shack. The waves outside had calmed down. She looked at the loaded gun, then she cracked the barrel open and took the bullet out. Not today.
Zh: 棚屋里一片漆黑。外面的海浪声已经平静了下来。她看着上好膛的枪，然后打开枪管，把子弹取了出来。今天不行。

En: It was your girlfriend. She cracked.
Zh: “你的女朋友出卖了你。面对质询她直接崩溃了。”

En: She smiles bitterly. "Nah, she didn't crack. She's a survivor. If she told you anything, it was to save herself..."
Zh: 她苦涩地笑了。“不，她不可能崩溃的，她是个幸存者。如果她真的跟你说了什么，那也是为了自救……”

En: Okay, call them the Hardie boys, then.
Zh: “好吧，那就叫他们哈迪兄弟吧。”

En: I did, didn't I. And now you've come for me. She scoffs. "But fuck them all the same."
Zh: “我确实说过，不是吗。所以现在你来抓我了。”她嗤之以鼻道。“不过，他们还是他妈的该死。”

En: That did make her forgive them -- a little.
Zh: 你这么说确实让她稍稍原谅了他们——那么一点点。

En: Police radio? You've been... following the case?
Zh: “警用无线电？你一直在……关注这个案子的进展？”

En: It's okay... We just want to... hnghhhhh... He struggles to finish the sentence.
Zh: “没事的……我们只想……唔……”他挣扎着想把话说完。

En: Wait... did she also seem high to you?
Zh: “等等……以你的标准来看她当时是不是已经嗨了？”

En: Oh yeah. *Super*. But I didn't think too much of it at first -- I'd seen her party hard before.
Zh: “何止，*超级*嗨。不过我一开始没想太多——我之前也见过她玩很疯的样子。”

En: This woman has seen people O.D. Klaasje wasn't quite there yet, so why worry?
Zh: 她见识过真正嗑药过量的人的样子，卡拉洁当时还没到那个程度，所以有什么好担心的呢？

En: Her idea?
Zh: “她的主意？”

En: Yeah. In cold blood. It really surprised me -- how quickly she was able to get a hold of herself once we got up there. It was like she was another person. The *party girl* was gone.
Zh: “是啊，特别冷血。真的把我给吓到了——我们上楼的时候她就已经稳定住情绪了。感觉像是变了个人一样，之前那个*派对女孩*不见了。”

En: You can see it: her lips, though still white, don't seem to tremble as much anymore. She moves with focus and deliberation.
Zh: 你能看见当时的场景：她的嘴唇，虽然依旧发白，但好像已然不再颤抖。她的一举一动从容而又专注。

En: Shower head? Resourceful...
Zh: “淋浴头？真是机智……”

En: It's especially bad suddenly. Felt like a vein exploded.
Zh: 感觉突然间变得很糟糕，就像血管爆炸了一样。

En: Who's 'everyone'? How do you know this?
Zh: “*大家*是指谁？你是从哪儿听来的？”

En: Bullshit, you're trying to... (wince grotesquely) "throw me off. No one says that."
Zh: “胡说，你是想……”（奇怪地抽搐），“误导我。根本就没人会这么说我。”

En: Everyone in Jamrock. The cops, the criminals... Why do you think I'm holed up in here with a goddamn death ray, waiting for you?
Zh: “‘大家’是指加姆洛克的所有人。警察啦，罪犯啦……要不然你以为我为什么会带着这台该死的死亡射线一起藏在这里，等着你找上门？”

En: Oh yes, they do. The cops, the criminals... Why do you think I'm holed up in here with a goddamn death ray, waiting for you?
Zh: “‘怎么会没人这么说你。警察啦，罪犯啦……要不然你以为我为什么会带着这台该死的死亡射线一起藏在这里，等着你找上门？”

En: If she knows *that* about you, she must know your real name too.
Zh: 如果她连*这个*都知道，那也一定知道你的真名。

En: Tell me, what's my name? If you know... that about me, you must... know my name.
Zh: “告诉我，我叫什么？如果你连这个……都知道，那你一定……也知道我的名字。”

En: Harry Du Bois, she replies quickly. "One corrupt motherfucker with the disco pants and the funny tie. Agent to La Puta Madre."
Zh: “哈里·杜博阿，”她飞快答道。“一个腐败的混蛋，穿着迪斯科裤子，还戴着一条搞笑的领带。马德雷教父的代理人。”

En: Don't let her scare you. She probably just made that name up on the spot.
Zh: 别被她唬住了，这个名字没准是她瞎蒙的。

En: No, no, it can't be that. You're not Harry Du Bois. Forget you heard any of this. There's no time to deal with this now.
Zh: 不，不，不是的。你不是哈里·杜博阿。忘了你听到的一切。现在没时间管这个。

En: So she knows your name. That doesn't mean you're on the take. Criminals make up bogeyman stories about cops all the time.
Zh: 看来她知道你的名字，但这也不意味着你真的有受贿的行为。罪犯就喜欢编些耸人听闻的故事来污蔑警察。

En: All of this just means that you're effective. Criminals know you and are scared of you.
Zh: 这一切只能代表你做事立竿见影。罪犯们都认得你，而且怕你。

En: I don't know... sounds pretty convincing to me.
Zh: 我不确定……听上去还挺可信的。

En: What did you *do* to this... Madre anyway?
Zh: “那你怎么*惹了*……那个马德雷了？”

En: You've been to my lorry. You think the biggest player in Jamrock appreciates competition? She pauses. "And now I have Harry Can-Opener in my lair. Fucking Titus..."
Zh: “你进我的货車里看过。你觉得加姆洛克最大的玩家喜欢竞争吗？”她停了一下。“结果现在人形开罐器哈里就出现在我的藏身之处了。该死的提图斯……”

En: You know what I did. I fucked him over. She pauses. "And now I have Harry Can-Opener in my lair. Fucking Titus..."
Zh: “你知道我做了什么。我骗了他。”她停了一下。“结果现在开罐器哈里就在我的藏身处现身了。该死的提图斯……”

En: Okay, other questions.
Zh: “好吧，换个别的问题。”

En: Yeah. Other questions.
Zh: “行啊，问吧。”

En: I thought we had an agreement, she thinks. *This* was not supposed to happen.
Zh: 我以为我们已经达成协议了，她想到。*这种事*真的不应该发生。

En: Wait, one thing. Possibly small but -- she said *you* rolled into town. Was that 'you' singular or plural? She might know something...
Zh: 等等，有一件事。也许很小，不过——她说*你*开车进城，那‘你’是一个人，还是有很多人？她也许知道些什么……

En: Yeah. You had your death squad with you. What happened to them, anyway?
Zh: “没错，你那时候带着你的杀手小队。话说回来，他们人呢？”

En: Who was in this... squad?
Zh: “那个……小队里都有谁？”

En: I don't... know what happened to them.
Zh: “我也不知道……他们去哪儿了。”

En: Sure you do, I bet they're just outside, waiting. She looks into the tunnel behind you. "I guess I'll take my chances."
Zh: “你不可能不知道，我敢打赌他们就在外面等着。”她看着你身后的隧道。“我想我之后要碰碰运气铤而走险看看了。”

En: She'll go out in a hail of bullets if she has to.
Zh: 如果别无选择的话，她会迎着枪林弹雨冲出去。

En: Well, it wasn't this scrawny dude. She nods toward the lieutenant. "You had two guys and a lady. The guys looked pretty buff. Lady wasn't bad either."
Zh: “呃，当时你队里没有这位瘦巴巴的老兄。”她朝警督点点头。“你带了两个男人，还有一个女人。男的看上去都很壮实，女的也不差。”

En: What else can you... tell me...
Zh: “你还有什么别的……能告诉我的吗……”

En: That's all... thank you.
Zh: “就这样吧……谢谢你。”

En: Hhhhhhhhzzzzzzzzzzz.......... friction lock set.. Zzzzzzzzzzz... Don't leave me here, please Celice!
Zh: 嗞嗞嗞嗞嗞嗞…….摩擦锁套装……嗞……别把我一个人留在这里，求你了塞利斯！

En: Yeah, that's right, I was just... testing you. They're right outside, with guns.
Zh: “是啊，没错，我刚才……在逗你玩呢。他们就在外面，荷枪实弹。”

En: I think I know them. They're in Martinaise...
Zh: “我好像认识他们。他们就在马丁内斯……”

En: No... that doesn't ring any bells.
Zh: “不……我一点印象都没有。”

En: I've faced worse. She shrugs.
Zh: “我见识过更糟糕的情况。”她耸耸肩。

En: She hasn't. She hasn't gone up against three armed people before. And she believed you.
Zh: 她才没有，她以前从没同时面对过三个武装分子。她相信了你的说法。

En: Well, this has been a great talk, really. But I think I'm going to hit the road now.
Zh: “好了，说句老实话，这次聊得挺愉快的，不过我得继续赶路了。”

En: She means it. You better make your move soon.
Zh: 她没在开玩笑，你要抓紧了。

En: Hhhhhhhzzzzzzz... L'Iramburde: starbursts and sunshine. 24 degrees centigrade...
Zh: 嗞嗞嗞嗞嗞……兰伯恩：星光与阳光。24摄氏度……

En: You've gotta act soon. This is getting *bad*.
Zh: 你动作最好快一点。情况越来越*糟糕*了。

En: Just try not to collapse.
Zh: 别晕过去了。

En: She knows about the tribunal?
Zh: 她知道审判的事？

En: Tribunal? What tribunal?
Zh: 审判？什么审判？

En: The pain is becoming unbearable now.
Zh: 疼痛变得愈发难忍。

En: Uhhm...
It's too late...
Zh: 太迟了……

En: This *is* how you talk her out of it. It's the only scenario in which she lives.
Zh: 只有这样你才能劝她放弃，这是唯一能让她活下去的方案。

En: You know.
Zh: 你知道的。

En: What options?
Zh: 什么选择？

En: Good call... The lieutenant is still unsteady on his feet.
Zh: “好主意……”警督的脚下还不是很稳。

En: She stares at you, frozen, the gun still in her mouth, eyes filled with dark intensity. Then something shifts in her.
Zh: 她盯着你，仿佛被冻住一样，枪还在她的嘴巴里，眼里充满漆黑的光芒。然后她体内有什么东西改变了。

En: Her neck and shoulders relax, and her grip on the gun loosens.
Zh: 她的脖子和肩膀放松下来，握枪的那只手也松动了。

En: You don't have to do this. You're not cornered. I'm letting you go.
Zh: “你这又是何必呢。你跟这个案子没关系，我可以放你走。”

En: Gratitude. Doubt. She's still ready to go...
Zh: 感激。怀疑。她还是想跑路……

En: Just fucking go, okay, before I change my mind!
Zh: “妈的，快走啊，在我改变主意之前！”

En: Day of miracles... she says, pulling the gun out of her mouth, eyes still fixed on you. Then she turns her gaze to the tunnel behind you. "I'll take it."
Zh: “奇迹降临之日……”她说道，然后一边眼睛牢牢盯着你，一边把枪管从嘴里拿了出来，然后瞥了一眼你身后的隧道。“我接受你的提议。”

En: Are you sure?
Zh: “你确定吗？”

En: I did my best.
Zh: “我尽力了。”

En: Thanks. (Rub your temples.)
Zh: “谢谢。”（摩挲你的两鬓。）

En: Yeah, I'm sure... He has to catch his breath. "I would have done the same had I not been incapacitated..."
Zh: “是的，我确定……”他得喘口气。“如果我没有丧失行动能力，一定也会做同样的事……”

En: What if we just let the murderer go?
Zh: “如果我们放凶手走会怎么样？”

En: I think she didn't do it.
Zh: “我觉得她不是凶手。”

En: I think she did it. But... I didn't want her to die.
Zh: “我觉得是她是凶手，但是……我不想她死。”

En: Really? That's... your takeaway? I'm *certain* she didn't do it. He looks around, then points to the back of the cavern. "Her tent. We should check it out."
Zh: “真的吗？这就是……你的判断？我*敢肯定*她不是凶手。”他看了看周围，然后指向洞穴深处。“她的帐篷。我们应该去检查一下。”

En: Her tent. We should check it out. He points to the back of the cavern.
Zh: 他看了看周围，然后指向洞穴深处。“她的帐篷。我们应该去检查一下。”

En: I have a feeling... we didn't. He looks around, then points to the back of the cavern. "Her tent. We should check it out."
Zh: “我有种直觉……我们放跑的那人不是凶手。”他看了看周围，然后指向洞穴背后。“她的帐篷。我们应该去检查一下。”

En: All things considered... The lieutenant is still reeling from the pain. "...that could've gone worse."
Zh: “考虑到种种因素……”警督还没从疼痛中缓过来。“……现在未必是最糟糕的结局。”

En: Glad to have been of assistance. She tosses her head back defiantly and turns down the machine. "Best of luck to you, officers -- Revachol's a bitch."
Zh: “很高兴能帮上忙。”她轻蔑地把头往后一仰，关小了机器。“祝你们好运了，警官——瑞瓦肖就是个婊子。”

En: Wait... shouldn't we go after her?
Zh: “等等……咱们不去追她吗？”

En: What now? (Conclude.)
Zh: “那现在怎么办呢？”（总结。）

En: No, I don't think she did it... She's someone else's case now.
Zh: “不用，我觉得凶手不是她……她跟咱们已经没关系了。”

En: It's too late now anyway. She's gone.
Zh: 再说现在已经来不及了。她已经走了。

En: Now we have to come up with a really good excuse... for why we walked right into a trap like that. This isn't going to look good in the report. He looks around, then points further into the cavern.
Zh: “现在我们必须想出个好理由……解释一下为什么我们会这么直直地自投罗网，否则写在报告里可就太难看了。”

En: Her tent! Maybe there's something in there.
Zh: 他看了看周围，然后指向洞穴深处。“她的帐篷！也许里面有些什么。”

En: I'm done. I'm not doing this anymore. (Conclude.)
Zh: “我受够了，不想再折腾了。”（总结。）

En: Okay... you said it. You needed to say it. Now it's been *said*, he says in a slow, measured voice. "We need to clean up."
Zh: “好吧……这可是你说的。你需要这么说。现在既然已经*说定*了，”他的声音缓慢而又慎重。“我们需要清理干净。”

En: No, I'm done. I'm not a cop anymore. [End it.]
Zh: “不要，我不干了，我不当警察了。”[结束它。]

En: Oh god...
Zh: “我的天……”

En: Okay. Okay. (Compose yourself.) "What do we do now?" (Move on.)
Zh: “好吧，好吧。”（让自己振作。）“咱们现在怎么办？”（继续。）

En: Fuck. The lieutenant stands, motionless, watching the blood pool in the sand.
Zh: “该死。”警督一动不动地站着，看着沙子上那一大滩血迹。

En: What did we just do?
Zh: “我们刚才到底做了什么？”

En: She killed herself. I failed to talk to her down.
Zh: “她自杀了。我没能劝住她。”

En: Lieutenant-yefreitor Du Bois, he intones. "Control your emotions. We did our *job*. This won't be the worst thing that happens on this case... believe me. You can't let this break you."
Zh: “高阶警督杜博阿，”他吟诵着。“请控制好你的情绪。我们只是在做自己的*本职工作*。这绝不会是这起案子的最低点……相信我。你不能被这种事击垮。”

En: Get a hold of yourself. He needs your support now too.
Zh: 控制好你自己。现在他需要你的支持。

En: You're going to need an ocean of booze to drown this out.
Zh: 你需要大量的酒精来吞没这一切。

En: We clean up. He looks around. "It may take days for processing to pick up her body. We need to move it somewhere..."
Zh: “我们把这里清理一下。”他看了看周围。“分析部的人可能要在几天之后才能来收殓她的尸体。我们需要找个地儿把她安置好……”

En: Why... hide the bullet, though?
Zh: “不过，为什么……要把子弹藏起来？”

En: This could've turned out pretty bad for me if you hadn't walked right into 25 bands of ultra-high frequencies.
Zh: “如果你们没像这样径直走入25超高频率波段的话，我可就倒大霉了。”

En: It's... it's our fault.
Zh: “这是……是我们的错。”

En: We're cops. Everything is our fault, if we let it be... He looks around.
Zh: “我们是警察。责任全在我们，要是我们放任不管……”他看了看四周。

En: She died. How can he be so... cold?
Zh: 她死了。他的反应怎么可以如此……冷淡？

En: This is the right thing to do -- clean up, keep a cool head.
Zh: 这样才对——把现场清理干净，同时保持冷静。

En: There is no coming back from this one. It will stay with you -- in nightmares.
Zh: 再也回不去了。它会一直伴着你——在你的噩梦中。

En: Despite his words, the lieutenant's barely keeping his composure; don't make it any harder.
Zh: 说归说，警督自己还是很难保持镇静；别再给他平添负担了。

En: *Detective*, he intones, "control your emotions. We did our *job*. This won't be the worst thing that happens to us on this case, believe me... You can't let this break you."
Zh: “*警探*，”他一字一句道。“管控好你的情绪。我们完成了*本职工作*，而这绝对不会是这起案子的最低点……相信我。你不能被这种事击垮。”

En: If she kills herself you'll get her gun...
Zh: 如果她自杀的话，你就能拿到她的枪了……

En: He squints into the darkness, "that tent there."
Zh: 他望向黑暗深处，“那边的帐篷。”

En: Her tent! Maybe there's something in there.
Zh: 他看了看周围，然后指向洞穴深处。“她的帐篷！也许里面有些什么。”

En: I have a feeling... we didn't. He looks around, then points to the back of the cavern. "Her tent. We should check it out."
Zh: “我有种直觉……我们放跑的那人不是凶手。”他看了看周围，然后指向洞穴背后。“她的帐篷。我们应该去检查一下。”

En: Her tent. We should check it out. He points to the back of the cavern.
Zh: 他看了看周围，然后指向洞穴深处。“她的帐篷。我们应该去检查一下。”

En: Really? That's... your takeaway? I'm *certain* she didn't do it. He looks around, then points to the back of the cavern. "Her tent. We should check it out."
Zh: “真的吗？这就是……你的判断？我*敢肯定*她不是凶手。”他看了看周围，然后指向洞穴深处。“她的帐篷。我们应该去检查一下。”

En: This must be how the twins heard the radio in their heads. They must have been standing by when she tested it.
Zh: 那对双胞胎肯定就是这样在脑子里听到广播的。她测试的时候他们一定就在附近。

En: This must be how the twins heard the radio in their heads. They must have been standing by when she tested it.
Zh: 那对双胞胎肯定就是这样在脑子里听到广播的。她测试的时候他们一定就在附近。

En: Suddenly, your entire body is paralysed. Aggressive white noise fills your skull -- a strange pain like you've never felt before. Through the static, you hear a woman's voice.
Zh: 顷刻间，你全身麻痹了。白噪声充斥着你的大脑——一阵前所未有的奇怪的痛楚接踵而至。透过静电流声，你听见了一个女人的声音。

En: Destroy the machine.
Zh: 破坏这台机器。

En: Tool equipped.
Zh: 工具已经装备上了。

En: Nudged it during drugs talk.
Zh: 趁谈论毒品的时候悄悄靠近。

En: Momentary window of clarity.
Zh: 片刻的清醒。

En: Convince her to put the gun down.
Zh: 说服她把枪放下。

En: What if *you* killed yourself?
Zh: 要是*你*自杀会怎么样？

En: Admitted Madre agent.
Zh: 承认自己是教父的代理人。

En: Said Tommy betrayed her.
Zh: 说是汤米背叛了她。

En: Said armed men outside.
Zh: 说外面有武装人员。

En: You did it. The Compressor lies broken on its side. It's quiet in your head again. It still hurts like hell, but...
Zh: 你做到了。压缩机已经抛锚了，你的脑子里又安静了，虽然还是疼得厉害，不过……

En: Say nothing. Try to cover your ears.
Zh: 什么也不说。试着捂住自己的耳朵。

En: The violent white noise becomes even more overwhelming closer to the Compressor. Feels like your brain is swelling up in your skull.
Zh: 一靠近压缩机，那阵猛烈的白噪音就变得更加汹涌。你感觉自己的大脑在颅骨中膨胀。

En: You said something about a tribunal...
Zh: “你好像说了些关于宣判的事……”

En: She's truly desperate -- she thinks she has no other options. You need to give her options.
Zh: 她彻底绝望了——她觉得自己已经走投无路了。你得提供一些选项给她。

En: You should know the words to say. You've been here yourself. So why is it not coming to you?
Zh: 你应该知道此刻该说些什么，你从前也有过相同的境遇，但为何脑中还一片空白？

En: Klaasje found it... weird you came up with a plan so quickly.
Zh: “你很快就想好了计划，对此卡拉洁觉得……有点奇怪。”

En: She's refusing to adopt the demeanour of a cornered animal. A leader with no one to lead -- she still wants to retain some control of the situation.
Zh: 她不想像头被逼入绝境的困兽一样行动。有如一只没了狼群的头狼——她还想保持对局势的一些掌控。

En: You're under arrest!
Zh: “你被捕了！”

En: See them fireworks, Kim? (Glance back.)
Zh: “看见机器冒烟了吗，金？”（向后看。）

En: She eyes you warily, as though gauging your sincerity.
Zh: 她警惕地盯着你，仿佛在估量你的真诚。

En: She looks at the machine, assessing the damage. Her hand trembles.
Zh: 她看着机器，评估着损坏程度。她的双手不住地颤抖着。

En: I can't take it anymore. Just go. (Don't attempt to destroy the compressor.)
Zh: “我承受不了了。算了吧。”（放弃破坏压缩机。）

En: It's pretty weird... that you came up with this plan right on the spot.
Zh: “你当下就想出了这个计划……这还真的挺奇怪的。”

En: (Whisper over your shoulder.) "You okay, Kim?"
Zh: （回头小声低语。）“你还好吧，金？”

En: Oh. She smiles sadly. "Well, I guess I always knew she was a *survivor* above all else."
Zh: “哦。”她露出了悲伤的笑容。“好吧，我想自己其实一直都知道她是个最优先考虑个人利益的*幸存者*。”

En: Don't know anything about it. No one's been around since I set up camp. But I'm sure I'm not the first vagabond to... Her voice trails off into white noise in your head. It feels like an aneurysm approaching.
Zh: “我什么都不知道，反正我在这里扎营之后就没人来过。不过我很肯定自己绝不是第一个在这里苟活的人……”她的声音越来越微弱，跟你脑袋中的白噪音混在了一起。感觉就像是动脉瘤在靠近。

En: Just a... place some comrades stopped through. A revolutionary bunker.
Zh: “就是个……公社同志会停下来歇歇脚的地方。一座大革命时期的碉堡。”

En: What? No. Faking a lynching was her idea.
Zh: “什么？不是。把现场伪造成私刑是她的主意。”

En: Oh. She smiles sadly. "I knew the kitten had claws, but not like this..."
Zh: “哦。”她露出了悲伤的笑容。“我虽然知道小猫也是有爪子的，但没想到会这样……”

En: You're right. Klaasje was the first... to share her suspicions.
Zh: “你说得对。卡拉洁是最先……跟我们透露她对你的怀疑的人。”

En: Your friend Klaasje really threw you to the wolves... said you shot Lely.
Zh: “是你的朋友卡拉洁，她把你推进了火坑里……她说是你开枪杀了莱利。”

En: When I threatened to arrest her, Klaasje broke... she told me everything.
Zh: “我威胁说要逮捕她的时候，卡拉洁崩溃了……她把一切都告诉我了。”

En: How did you manage to... come up with a plan? So quickly?
Zh: “你到底是怎么……想出这个计划的？而且还这么快？”

En: Who ratted me out, by the way? Was it Titus? No, she hesitates, "he wouldn't have broken first..."
Zh: “顺便问一句，到底是谁出卖的我？是提图斯吗？不对，”她犹豫了一下，“第一个招了的不会是他……”

En: She looks shaken. She wasn't surprised to be ratted out -- but *framed*?
Zh: 她看起来有些动摇。她并不意外会有人把自己给供出来——但是万万没料到会被人*诬陷*。

En: So she says she didn't do it -- and she doesn't trust you. Is it you specifically or the Citizens Militia that she distrusts?
Zh: 她说了不是她干的——而且她不相信你。她是不相信你这个人，还是整个公民武装？

En: Klaasje said... you seemed to have a plan... prepared when you got there.
Zh: “卡拉洁说……你到她那儿的时候……好像就已经把计划想好了。”

En: I know you're feeling pretty uncomfortable right now. Don't move too much or fight it. That'll just make it worse, says the shadowy figure by the machine.
Zh: “我知道你现在感觉不是很舒服。动作不要太大，也不要去抵抗。这样只会让情况更糟糕，”机器旁边的影子开了口。

En: Do you know about the... bunker... next door?
Zh: “你知道……这个碉堡吗……就在隔壁？”

En: Fine, if you really want to talk, I can dial it down. I've also got a gun, by the way. She steps reluctantly out of the shadows. The pain lessens.
Zh: “好吧，如果你真想谈，我可以把机器调小。顺便提一嘴，我手上还有把枪呢。”她不情愿地从阴影中走了出来。痛苦减轻了。

En: Go ahead, then. (Let her do it.)
Zh: “那好吧。”（由着她这么做。）

En: ...you made it look... like he'd been hanged...
Zh: “……你把现场布置得……就像他是被吊死的一样……”

En: What bunker?
Zh: “什么碉堡？”

En: I saw *you* roll into town. I wasn't about to stick around for questioning by a goddamn La Puta Madre agent.
Zh: “我看见*你*进城了。我可不打算干坐着等马德雷教父该死的代理人找上门来。”

En: Who killed the merc... if it wasn't you?
Zh: “那杀了那个雇佣兵又会是谁……如果不是你的话？”

En: The gun she's carrying is a two-barrelled front-loader -- not like the murder weapon.
Zh: 她手中握着的是一支双管前膛枪——跟凶器并不一致。

En: How should I know? As I keep saying, he already had a bullet in his head when I got to him, and there hasn't been any useful gossip over the radio...
Zh: “我怎么会知道？我不是说了吗，我发现他的时候他的脑袋已经中枪了，而且收音机里也没听到什么有用的小道消息……”

En: The voice coming through the whirlwind of pain is not malicious. She doesn't want to hurt you -- but she has to.
Zh: 穿过痛苦漩涡的人声并没有恶意。她不想伤害你——但她必须这么做。

En: What's happening to me?
Zh: “我这是怎么了？”

En: Are you gonna... kill me too?
Zh: “所以你打算……也杀了我吗？”

En: Help, Kim, help! My brain's on fire!
Zh: “救命啊，金，救命！我的脑袋着火了！”

En: Can't say it's a pleasure, officer. I was really hoping *not* to make your acquaintance. But -- here we are.
Zh: “我没办法说很高兴认识你，警官。我真心希望自己能够*不认识*你。但是——咱们还是见面了。”

En: That boy's really gotta learn to keep his trap shut. And it's not a bloody pale emitter. It doesn't emit *pale*...
Zh: “那个小子真该好好学学该怎么管住自己的臭嘴。而且这不是什么鬼灰域发射器。它不会发射*灰域*……”

En: You should probably check on Kim. That's what good partners do.
Zh: 你也许应该确认一下金的状况。好搭档就会这么做。

En: Doesn't wish to hurt you? Not according to your ear canals... wait, no, not even your ear canals -- this is going directly into your neural pathways.
Zh: 不想伤害你？你的耳道可不这么想……等等，不，甚至不是你的耳道——这台机器是直接冲着你的听觉神经去的。

En: No, I didn't do it. I only helped stage the lynching. Though I doubt that makes much of a difference to *you*.
Zh: “不，我没杀人，我只是帮忙布置了私刑现场。不过我怀疑这么说对*你*而言也没什么区别。”

En: She asked me to come upstairs -- the merc she'd been going with was lying on the bedroom floor, dead. I knew she couldn't get the authorities involved. So, yeah...
Zh: “她要我上楼去找她——那个跟她一起的雇佣兵就躺在卧室地板上，死了。我知道她不希望让当局介入。所以，没错……”

En: As she says the word "officer," you feel a spike in the agony. It sounds like the entire radio frequency range is screaming directly into your neural pathways.
Zh: 当她说出“警官”这个词的时候，你感觉到一阵尖锐的剧痛。听起来就像整个射频波段直接朝着你的神经通路尖叫。

En: No, I really didn't. She's not that easy to read. I just assumed she'd done too much blow. It wouldn't be a first for her. But -- no such luck. She was in some *deep* shit.
Zh: “不，我真的没有。她不是个容易被看透的人。我只是假设她嗑过头了。对于她来说，肯定不是第一次。不过——她没那份运气。她真是*深陷*困境了。”

En: If you've got something *really* important to say, you can do it through the white noise.
Zh: “如果你*真的*有什么重要的话要说，可以在白噪音里头说。”

En: Klaasje... said you knew something was wrong immediately.
Zh: “卡拉洁……她说你当下就知道出问题了。”

En: Cover your ears!
Zh: 捂住你的耳朵！

En: I still have a *case* to make... let's continue...
Zh: “我还有个*案子*要破……我们继续吧……”

En: Did you shoot Lely?
Zh: “你朝莱利开枪了吗？”

En: And what did she say?
Zh: “那她说了什么？”

En: Look back at Kim.
Zh: 回头看着金。

En: Keep your eyes on Ruby.
Zh: 盯着露比。

En: Just a bunker. Have you been there?!
Zh: “就是个碉堡。你去过那里吗？”

En: No, I'm not going to fucking kill you. If that had been my plan, you'd be dead already. All things considered, I'm being pretty reasonable here.
Zh: “我他妈的不会杀你的，如果我真有这个打算，你现在早就没命了。考虑到现在的情况，我已经很讲道理了。”

En: Mmm... The lieutenant clutches his head as his eyes roll back into his skull.
Zh: “唔……”警督抓着自己的脑袋，眼珠上翻。

En: The torment Lieutenant Kitsuragi is experiencing is worse than your own. He's trying to resist, but there's no way of knowing when he will recover.
Zh: 曷城警督此刻忍受的痛苦比你还要严重。他试着去抵抗，但根本无从知道何时才能解脱。

En: (Yell through the static.) "You're under arrest!"
Zh: （在静电声中大喊。）“你被捕了！”

En: Yeah, they love sending people to faraway places...
Zh: “是啊，他们喜欢把人派去很远的地方……”

En: Really now? Check this out! She turns the dial in her hand.
Zh: “你认真的吗？那就尝尝这个吧！”她拧动手中的转盘。

En: You're overwhelmed with a new surge of violent static. It feels like a blood vessel exploded in your brain.
Zh: 一股全新的强电流向你袭来，你感觉大脑里的血管好像要爆了。

En: She sits down with a drink, trying to steady her nerves. So I grab a seat next to her.
Zh: “她端着一杯酒坐下，想要稳定自己的情绪。然后我就在她旁边坐下了。”

En: Right behind you, officer. Eyes closed, the lieutenant is doubled over. He's still alive and breathing.
Zh: “我在你身后呢，警官。”警督紧闭双眼，弯腰蜷缩着。他还活着，还有呼吸。

En: The torment Lieutenant Kitsuragi is experiencing is worse than your own, but he's slowly beginning to recover.
Zh: 曷城警督正在承受的痛苦比你还要严重，但他已经慢慢开始恢复了。

En: Nah. That I'm not planning to shoot you is the extent of the courtesy I can extend to La Puta Madre at present.
Zh: “不。我没打算朝你开枪，这已经是我现在能对马德雷教父展示的最大礼遇。”

En: Alright, don't kill yourself over it. I was shooting the shit with Hardie and the boys over a few beers, like always. Then Klaasje comes in, all pale and shuddering...
Zh: “好吧，别把自己折腾死了。我那时候跟哈迪兄弟们喝了几杯啤酒，像往常一样瞎扯淡，然后卡拉洁就进来了，面色苍白，全身颤抖……”

En: Fuck yeah, I'm a La Puta Madre agent... You better let me go.
Zh: “没错，我就是马德雷教父的代理人……你最好放我走。”

En: Agent... Is La Puta Madre some kind of travel agency?
Zh: “代理人……马德雷教父是什么旅行社吗？”

En: The woman runs past you and disappears into the darkness of the tunnel.
Zh: 女人从你身边跑过，消失在黑暗的隧道里。

En: Don't focus on the pain -- focus on doing your job. Tell her she's under arrest.
Zh: 把注意力从疼痛上转移走——想想你的工作。告诉她说她被逮捕了。

En: The communist hideout back there...
Zh: “康米主义者的藏身地就在后面……”

En: Fat Angus said you had a pale emitter...
Zh: “胖子安格斯说你有一台灰域发射器……”

En: What happened... Sunday night? Tell me your version.
Zh: “到底发生了什么？周日晚上？跟我说说你的版本。”

En: I'm using a pale latitude compressor. You and your partner have been caught in its field.
Zh: “我用的是一台灰域维度压缩机。你和你的搭档现在就困在它的场域里。”

En: 236189281... If you're looking for a deal on mattresses... SUHSUHSUHSPEEDFRRRR... 23567... 32971047302819... Oh Rosaline, oh Rosaline...
No, buddy, that's not going to help. You can't shield yourself from this -- it's an entirely *new* type of experience. Way worse than all the previous ones.
Zh: 不，伙计，这么做没用的，你是没办法屏蔽它的——这是一种*全新*的体验，你就从没遭过这样的罪。

En: Hhhhhzzzzzzzzssss... 136841... hhhhhzzzzzzzzzzssss... 37891303... hhhhhzzzzzzzsssss... hzzzzsssss...
Zh: “嗞嗞嗞嗞嗞嗞嗞……136841……嘶嘶嘶嘶嘶嘶嘶嘶嘶嘶……37891303……嗞嗞嗞嗞嗞嗞……嘶嘶嘶嘶嘶嘶嘶嘶……”

En: What's this goddamned *pale* anyway?
Zh: “话说，那个*灰域*是什么鬼东西？”

En: I hate to interrupt the deafening pain you're in, but Joyce said it was possible to force dimensions by using radio waves. This must be one of the devices used to send radio signals across the pale.
Zh: 我也不想打扰你现在正在承受的这震耳欲聋痛苦，但是乔伊斯说过——使用无线电波去强加维度是有可能的。这一定就是向灰域传输无线电信号的设备之一。

En: Precisely. This is an industrial-strength paraboloid. It's meant for forcing dimensions on something that doesn't *have* them. Needless to say, the frequencies used are... out of this world.
Zh: “没错。这是一个工业级的抛物面反射器。专门用于将维度强加到某种*没有*维度的东西上。所以理所当然的，它所使用的频率……并不属于这个世界。”

En: At the upper limit is the large prime number generator station. It's used specifically for pale latitude compression. That's why you may be hearing some numbers. But you might also hear -- or think you're hearing -- local radio chatter.
Zh: “频率达到上限时这台机器就相当于是大型素数生成站，专门用于压缩灰域维度。这就是你可能会听到一些数字的原因。不过，你同时也可能会听见——或者你会自以为听到——本地电台调频的说话声。”

En: How did you get your hands on this thing?
Zh: “你是怎么弄到这玩意的？”

En: Will I stay like this forever?
Zh: “我会一直这个样子吗？”

En: I built it myself. She nods toward her torture device.
Zh: “我自己造的。”她朝着那台刑具点了点头。

En: No -- once I shut down the compressor, the pain will end. She hesitates. "It may take a few minutes for you to steady yourself, though -- it's a bit like waking out of a very confusing dream."
Zh: “不会——只要我关掉压缩机，痛苦就会结束。”她犹豫着。“不过你可能需要花上几分钟才能稳住自己——感觉有点像是刚做了场昏昏沉沉的梦。”

En: And she's proud of it, too. As she ought to. This is way beyond your abilities.
Zh: 而且她也对此感到很自豪。她也有理由感到自豪，你就造不出这样的东西。

En: Damn... this... The lieutenant clutches his head, grimacing.
Zh: 警督抓着自己的头，表情十分痛苦。“该死……这个……”

En: Have you... *experienced* the compressor yourself?
Zh: “你有没有……亲自*体验*过这台压缩机？”

En: Yeah, I stuck my head in there before using it on you. It seemed like the *ethical* thing to do. Can't say that I enjoyed it. The field was weaker, but I can imagine what you're going through.
Zh: “有啊，在对你使用之前，我自己也把脑袋伸进去过，感觉这样做比较*合乎科学伦理*。不敢说是很愉快的体验，我试的时候开的档位比现在还低，不过我可以想象你现在正经受的痛苦。”

En: A man in a white suit walks through a garden coaxed from soil that had once been covered in asphalt -- a city block closed off from the rest of the city by dark buildings.
Zh: 一个穿着白色西装的男人走过一个花园，里面的泥土曾经覆盖在沥青下面——黑暗的建筑物把这个街区和其他街区隔开了。

En: Right, sure. You don't know.
Zh: “哦，当然了。你什么都不知道。”

En: A pale latitude compressor is used to sort of... make the pale more manageable. With a lot of these, you can force a radio signal grid on the pale -- literally crunch the distance across it.
Zh: “灰域维度压缩机是用来……更好地控制灰域的。如果有很多这玩意，你就能把一个无线电信号网格强加在灰域上——从字面意义上讲，就是压缩它的距离。”

En: Signals are relayed across a series of repeater stations fixed to buoys. She pauses. "Not a fun job, manning those stations. All alone out there in the pale, people lose their minds in just a few years."
Zh: “信号通过一系列中继站来完成传递。”她停顿了一下。“驻守在那些中继站里可不是什么有意思的工作，人类独自呆在灰域之中的话，不需要几年就会失去理智的。”

En: So, what we are experiencing is... a concentration of radio waves... He gestures toward something with great effort.
Zh: “所以，我们现在听到的这些……是无线电波的集合……”他费了好大力气比划了一下周围。

En: Is that a joke or a threat? I'm guessing both.
Zh: “你现在是在说笑呢，还是在威胁我？我猜两者都有吧。”

En: I'm no one's... agent. I don't even know who La Puta Madre is!
Zh: “我不是任何人的……代理人。我甚至连马德雷教父是谁都不知道！”

En: La Puta Madre... I've heard of La Puta Madre... He's dangerous... right?
Zh: “马德雷教父……我听说过马德雷教父……他是个危险人物……对吗？”

En: Yeah? What?
Zh: “嗯？什么？”

En: Sounds like she knew someone who used to man one of those stations. But she won't tell you about her. Boy, you're one empathetic police officer to have guessed even that right now.
Zh: 听上去她似乎认识在那些中继站里驻守过的人。不过她肯定不会告诉你的。兄弟，作为一个善于察言观色的警察，你现在心里清楚着呢。

En: But... even if this is true...  The lieutenant forces himself to finish the sentence: "Weren't you worried this 'lynching' might lead to..."
Zh: “但是……就算你说的都是真的……”警督强迫自己把剩下的话说完了。“你那个时候就不担心把现场处理成‘私刑’会引发……”

En: ...war? She purses her lips. "The thought crossed my mind. But the mercenary's death was going to have repercussions either way. Although, the way things are going..."
Zh: “……战争吗？”她咬起嘴唇。“我想过。但那个雇佣兵的死亡无论如何都会产生后果，尽管现在事情的走向有点……”

En: Eh, fuck it. She shrugs. "I'm not responsible for these people after what they did to me."
Zh: 她耸耸肩。“管它呢，见鬼去吧。在他们对我做出那些事之后，我也不需要对他们负责。”

En: She doesn't want to talk about this, but not because she has something to hide -- she doesn't want the guilt.
Zh: 她不想提这个，不是因为她有什么需要隐瞒——只是不想感到内疚罢了。

En: Do you know something about the tribunal... the mercenaries are planning?
Zh: “你听说了所谓审判的事吗……就是那群雇佣正在筹划的事？”

En: She chuckles. "*Tribunal* is an awfully fancy word for what they're going to do. But, yeah, I was listening to their frequency. They've finished their 'investigation.'"
Zh: 她咯咯笑了起来。“*审判*这个词相对于他们实际上想做的事来说也忒好听了点。话说回来，没错，我监听过他们的频道。他们的‘调查’已经完成了。”

En: Now they're going to put on a show before meting out punishment. She pauses, biting her lip. "Even though those assholes gave me up... I'm worried for the boys."
Zh: “现在他们打算在行刑前先做一场秀。”她停了一下，咬紧嘴唇。“尽管那帮孙子出卖了我……我还是很担心他们。”

En: She sighs. "Yeah. The mercenaries are about to set up a tribunal and execute someone for killing Lely."
Zh: 她叹了口气。“是啊。那群佣兵打算私设公堂，处决杀害莱利的人。”

En: How do you know this?
Zh: “你怎么知道的？”

En: I was listening in on their frequency. She pauses, biting her lip. "Even though those assholes gave me up... I'm worried for the boys."
Zh: “我监听了他们的频道。”她停了一下，咬住自己的嘴唇。“虽然那帮孙子出卖了我……我还是很担心他们。”

En: Squirm in pain.
Zh: 痛苦地蠕动。

En: Hold very still.
Zh: 保持静止。

En: 875263... 23621837... for that special someone...
I know it's bad -- try not to move.
Zh: “我知道这种感觉很难受——别乱动了。”

En: There, there, I told you it's best not to move.
Zh: “看吧，我说的吧，我都告诉过你最好别动了。”

En: And you -- watch out for yourselves. You seem to have a habit of walking into traps.
Zh: “还有你——最好以后多留点神。你好像有自投罗网的习惯。”

En: They're planning to set up a trap for us?
Zh: “他们已经设好陷阱等着我们上钩了吗？”

En: Why is everyone always against me?
Zh: “为什么每个人都喜欢跟我作对？”

En: I'm guessing you don't know that a third mercenary has come to town just in time for the tribunal -- now you do.
Zh: “我猜你应该不知道还有第三个雇佣兵已经进城了，正好能赶上宣判——不过现在你知道了。”

En: I'm asking myself that right now too. Anyway, I'm guessing you don't know that a third mercenary has come to town just in time for the tribunal -- now you do.
Zh: “我也在问自己这个问题。总之，我猜你应该不知道还有第三个雇佣兵已经进城了，正好能赶上宣判——不过现在你知道了。”

En: A third... Oh, this is bad.
Zh: “还有第三个……哎，真下可糟糕了。”

En: As I said -- watch out. She nods.
Zh: 她点点头。“我不是说了吗——小心点。”

En: If one hornet can kill 40 honeybees in a minute, then three hornets should be able to kill 120 honeybees in a minute... Or more, since they'll also be covering one another.
Zh: 如果一只大黄蜂可以在一分钟内杀死40只蜜蜂，那三只大黄蜂一分钟就能杀120只蜜蜂……甚至更多，因为他们还会团队配合。

En: Three mercenaries, no doubt armed to the teeth, hungry for vengeance... You've only got muzzle loaders. This isn't looking good.
Zh: 三个雇佣兵，毫无疑问一定是全副武装，渴望复仇……而你只有前膛枪。这看起来不太妙啊。

En: The lieutenant hunches, recovering. "All good, officer. Be careful."
Zh: 警督弯着腰，正在慢慢缓过来。“我还好，警官。千万小心。”

En: Ah, fuck it. She puts the barrel of the gun into her mouth.
Zh: “啊，去他娘的吧。”她把枪管塞进嘴里。

En: What are you *doing*?
Zh: “你在*干嘛*？”

En: Problem-solving, she mutters.
Zh: “解决问题，”她嘟囔着。

En: Ma'am, put the gun down. That's not the solution to your problems. You are...
Zh: “女士，把枪放下。这样是不能解决问题的。你……”

En: Oh, yes, it is.
Zh: “哦，这当然能解决问题。”

En: Maybe I can still talk her out of it?
Zh: 也许我应该劝劝她？

En: Please. (Put your hands up.) "Just walk away."
Zh: “拜托，”（高举双手，）“你走吧。”

En: I wish I could just let you go, but I have to follow protocol.
Zh: “我也希望可以就这样放你走，但我必须遵守规定。”

En: She pulls the trigger before you finish your sentence.
Zh: 你的话音未落，她就扣动了扳机。

En: You watch as her brains trickle out through her neon hair.
Zh: 你看着她的脑浆从霓虹色的头发中飞溅而出。

En: She runs past you, then past the lieutenant, and disappears into the darkness of the tunnel.
Zh: 她从你身边、接着是警督身边飞奔而过，最后消失在黑暗的隧道里。

En: Why am I standing here like an idiot?
Zh: 为什么我像个白痴一样的傻站着？

En: Because you think suicide might be a good course of action for you too. Her finger squeezes the trigger now...
Zh: 因为你觉得自杀对你来说也是种不错的做法。现在她的手指已经握紧扳机了……

En: Because you've been going around pointing a gun at yourself, trying to answer the same question in vain. Her finger squeezes the trigger now...
Zh: 因为你也曾经拿枪指着自己，试图找到同样的问题的答案，但一切都是徒劳。现在她的手指已经握紧扳机了……

En: Because you've misread the situation from the beginning. And now her finger squeezes the trigger...
Zh: 因为你从一开始就错估了形式。现在她的手指已经握紧扳机了……

En: But -- she doesn't deserve to die like this!
Zh: 但是——她不该就这样平白无故地死去！

En: I can't stop her...
Zh: 我阻止不了她……

En: I was drunk! I didn't mean it!...
Zh: 我当时说的是醉话！我不是故意的！……

En: Come on, one idea, I need one idea!
Zh: 来吧，点子，我需要个好点子！

En: She flashes you an incredulous grin. Then she exhales sharply, shakes her head, and pulls the trigger.
Zh: 她朝你露出一丝怀疑的笑容，然后重重呼出一口气，摇了摇头，紧接着就扣动了手中的扳机。

En: I'm sure I'm supposed to know... but I lost my memory recently.
Zh: “我确定自己理应是知道的……但是我最近失忆了。”

En: Could you... tell me about it?
Zh: “你能不能……跟我说说？”

En: Let's... talk about something else.
Zh: “咱们……还是聊聊别的吧。”

En: I'm sorry... I know I'm supposed to know all about... this, but I lost my memory recently...
Zh: “抱歉……我确定自己理应是知道的……但是我最近失忆了。”

En: Yes, of course. Are you... *scared* yet?
Zh: “哦，我当然是了。你……现在是不是*怕*了啊？”

En: No, that was a real question!
Zh: “不，那才是真正的问题所在！”

En: Yeah, sure.
Zh: “好啊，当然了。”

En: I'm sure La Puta Madre himself will explain it all to you soon enough.
Zh: “我确定马德雷教父很快会亲自向你解释的。”

En: Of you? No, not really.
Zh: “怕你？不，一点都不。”

En: But she couldn't have known I was on the coast. How did you find me?
Zh: “但是她不可能知道我躲在海岸区。你是怎么找到我的？”

En: Titus and his boys, ma'am. They told us you were on the coast.
Zh: “是提图斯和他的兄弟们说的，女士。他们告诉我们你躲在海岸区。”

En: Even now, Kim is a paragon of professionalism: he's trying to make a clean cut of telling her she was betrayed.
Zh: 即使在现在的情形下，金也是职业精神的典范：他想要清楚明了地告知她——她被背叛了。

En: Your... own boys told us you were on the coast.
Zh: “你的……那群兄弟告诉我们你在海岸的。”

En: Titus told me. It took some convincing...
Zh: “是提图斯告诉我的。我嘴都快说破皮了……”

En: Your first guess wasn't entirely off...
Zh: “你的第一个猜测并不算完全错误……”

En: *My* boys?
Zh: “*我的*兄弟？”

En: Yeah, the ones you poached from Titus. And Titus himself.
Zh: “是啊，你从提图斯那里挖走的。还有提图斯自己。”

En: Ruby boys... Formerly the Hardie boys.
Zh: “露比兄弟……前身是哈迪兄弟。”

En: She looks at you incredulously. "What is this? I thought Titus and I had a decent collegial relationship..."
Zh: 她满脸疑惑地看着你。“你在说什么？我以为提图斯和我只是正经的同事关系……”

En: Oh, it doesn't even matter. How can they be *my* boys when they're not even my friends. And those guys *liked* me -- I know it. If this is what happens to people whom people like...
Zh: “哦，这根本不重要。他们对我来说甚至连朋友都算不上，更别说是*自家*兄弟了。至于有人*喜欢*我这件事——我太知道了。如果一个人有人喜欢就会遇上这种事……”

En: She looks at you incredulously. "What? I don't even understand what you're trying to get at here."
Zh: 她满脸疑惑地看着你。“什么？我都不明白你到底想表达什么。”

En: A dull despair is creeping into her voice.
Zh: 她的嗓音里隐隐透露出绝望。

En: ...how the fuck do the rest of you get by?
Zh: “……你们其他人到底是怎么他妈的办到的？”

En: Does she mean that you're not a person whom other people like?
Zh: 她是不是在暗示说你是没人喜欢的那类人？

En: Aw, fuck. Took some convincing my ass. And those guys *liked* me -- I know it. If this is what happens to people whom people like...
Zh: “呕，拉倒吧，嘴说破个屁。有人*喜欢*我什么——我太知道了。如果一个人有人喜欢就会遇上这种事……”

En: She pauses, taking it in. "Well, fuck. And those guys *liked* me -- I know it. If this is what happens to people whom people like..."
Zh: 她停下来消化了一会儿。“呃，去他妈的。有人*喜欢*我什么——我太知道了。如果一个人有人喜欢就会遇上这种事……”

En: Rows and rows of poppies, most of which have lost their pink bloom, flank his steps. The man looks around, then up at the sky; sighs.
Zh: 他漫步着，两侧是一排排的罂粟花，大部分粉红色的花朵已经凋谢。男人看了看周围，然后抬头望向天空；一声叹息。

En: Taking out a knife, he crouches at the end of one flower bed. He scores a seedpod, very gently. Milky sap begins to ooze out. The pain comes over you again...
Zh: 他掏出一把刀，蹲伏在一个花坛的尽头。他在一只荚果上划了一刀，乳白色的汁液渗露而出。痛苦再度将你包围……

En: If you didn't kill him... why hide?
Zh: “既然你没杀他……为什么还要躲起来？”

En: What do you mean... La Puta Madre agent?
Zh: “你说马德雷教父的代理人……是什么意思？”

En: Yes -- you. She looks at you quizzically. "Everyone says you're his peone. His 'Human Can Opener.'"
Zh: 她疑惑地看着你。“是的——说的就是你。大家都说你是他的傀儡，他的‘人形开罐器。’”

En: Through the sudden sharp pain in your head, you hear the lieutenant mumble something to himself.
Zh: 你的大脑突然感到一阵尖锐的剧痛，你听见警督在喃喃些什么。

En: Fucking hell and "Why me?" you hear through the white noise.
Zh: “真他妈该死”，还有“为什么是我？”透过白噪音，你听到这两句话。

En: He was still warm, but the blueish light coming through the broken window made him look as though he'd been dead for a good long while...
Zh: “他的身体还是温热的，但在透过破掉的窗户射进来的青色灯光的照射下他看起来好像已经死了很久……”

En: Those rings around her eyes, her tired voice -- she's been staying up late listening in on the conversations criss-crossing Martinaise.
Zh: 她眼周布满黑眼圈，声音疲惫——她熬夜到很晚，一直在听着跟马丁内斯范围内的各种电波。

En: Who hasn't? She shrugs. "You know, I can still see him there, in Klaasje's room, lying on his side."
Zh: “谁不是呢？”她耸耸肩。“要知道，我现在还能看见他，就在卡拉洁的房间里，侧躺着。”

En: No, wait, finally, it comes to you, a way to connect with her:
Zh: 不，等等，你终于想到了，你跟她的共同点：

En: I know your pain! I've been let down by women too.
Zh: “我明白你的痛苦！我也被女人伤害过。”

En: A smirk crosses her face as she watches you stand there, mute. Then she exhales sharply, shakes her head, and pulls the trigger.
Zh: 她看着你站在那里，不发一言，脸上闪过幸灾乐祸的笑容。接着她重重呼出一口气，摇了摇头，扣动了手中的扳机。

En: I do it by... asking questions. And I have some for you.
Zh: “我靠的就是……问问题啊。而且我也有问题要问你。”

En: Wait, wasn't it... you who called me the *human can opener*? It's not personal. I... opened them up.
Zh: “等等，管我叫我*人形开罐器*的……不就是你吗？不是针对你啊。所以我……就像开罐子一样让他们开口了。”

En: Like what? She adjusts her grip on her gun. "I already told you I didn't do it."
Zh: “比如说？”她调整了一下持枪的姿势。“我已经告诉过你了，人不是我杀的。”

En: Honestly, I don't know how I do it... I just stumbled in here. Can you... please explain this shit to me?
Zh: “坦白说，我也不知道自己是怎么找到这里的……我就是凑巧经过。你能不能……给我解释一下现在到底是怎么回事？”

En: What shit? She adjusts her grip on her gun. "I told you I didn't do it. Sounds pretty simple to me."
Zh: “怎么回事？”她调整了一下持枪的姿势。“我已经告诉过你了，人不是我杀的。我觉得挺好懂的。”

En: A strong moral compass. She still wants the opportunity to make a case for herself.
Zh: 强大的道德感。她仍想找个机会为自己辩护。

En: She asked me to help her drag him into the shower so she could wind the shower head around his neck. To fake lividity. Then she dressed him while I went to get the Hardie boys.
Zh: “她让我帮忙把他拖进浴室，然后她就能用淋浴头的线缠绕在他的脖子周围，勒出青紫色的勒痕。然后她帮他穿好衣服，而我去找哈迪兄弟。”

En: That's... bad -- that she'd be so calm...
Zh: “那还真是……不妙——她居然能如此镇静……”

En: I don't believe you!
Zh: “我不信！”

En: Yeah, I wasn't sure whether to admire her -- or be disturbed.
Zh: “是啊，我也不知道到底是该钦佩她——还是该感到不安。”

En: I really don't care which one of us you think was more at fault for the 'lynching.' Neither of us killed him, though.
Zh: “不管你觉得我俩到底谁更应该为‘私刑’负责，我真的都无所谓，反正人不是我们杀的。”

En: How can you be sure... Klaasje didn't kill him herself?
Zh: “你为什么这么肯定……他不是卡拉洁杀的？”

En: As I keep telling you cops, we didn't hear anything downstairs. No gunshot, nothing.
Zh: “警官，我不是说了好多遍了吗，我们在楼下什么也没听见。没有枪声，什么也没有。”

En: The shot was made from a distance -- otherwise we would have heard it downstairs. Besides, she had good reason to not draw attention to herself.
Zh: “子弹是从远处射进来的——否则我们在楼下应该能听到枪声才对。再说了，她有充分的动机不去主动招惹是非引人注意。”

En: Do you... think she killed Lely... herself?
Zh: “你觉得……是不是她亲自动手……杀了莱利……？”

En: Do you... think she might have killed the merc herself?
Zh: “那你……觉得雇佣兵会不会是她亲手杀的？”

En: As I keep telling you cops, we didn't hear anything downstairs. No gunshot, nothing.
Zh: “警官，我不是说了好多遍了吗，我们在楼下什么也没听见。没有枪声，什么也没有。”

En: And potentially bring attention to herself? No. Besides, the shot was made from a distance -- otherwise we would have heard it downstairs.
Zh: “然后惹得一身骚？不可能。再说，子弹是从远处射进来的——否则我们在楼下应该能听到枪声才对。”

En: Klaasje. She's clearly a gender traitor.
Zh: “卡拉洁，她显然背叛了自己的性别群体。”

En: It's like a thousand radio stations are being blasted into your head all at once, but her words are the only ones you can make out.
Zh: 就像是有一千个无线电台同时向你的脑袋轰炸，但一片嘈杂中你唯独能辨认出她的话语。

En: Take out all the good parts of pyrholidon, leave all the bad parts, and make a brain-bomb. In a constant state of exploding. That's this.
Zh: 拿出嚯浣酮，取其精华去其糟粕后做一个大脑炸弹，使其处于恒定的爆炸状态之中。大功告成。

En: This must be the pale emitter Fat Angus mentioned.
Zh: 这一定就是胖子安格斯提到的灰域发射器。

En: The explosion of static you're hearing -- it's the ULAN frequency.
Zh: 你听到的静电爆炸声——就是ULAN频率。

En: I saw your equations... It's the ULAN frequency!
Zh: “我见过你写的方程式了……这是ULAN频率。”

En: *Saw my equations*? She snorts. "You've been sniffing through my lorry, right? I expected as much. I *am* a bit surprised you knew what you were looking at..."
Zh: “你*见过我的方程式*？”她吸吸鼻子。“你翻过我的货車了，对吧？这点我倒不意外，但你居然知道自己看到的是什么，这点*倒是*出乎我的意料……”

En: Blasted from that pale emitter Fat Angus mentioned.
Zh: 从胖子安格斯提到的灰域发射器里发出来的。

En: You're using one of those devices used to send signals through the pale on me!
Zh: “你现在正在对我使用的是那种跨灰域传递信号的机器！”

En: Wow, check out the big brains on you. She sounds impressed. "You're not entirely wrong..."
Zh: “哇哦，瞧瞧你这颗发达的脑袋。”她听起来对你的表现颇为赞许。“你说的有几分在点子上……”

En: She's been holed up in here for a while with no one to talk to. Keep her talking, and you just might get an opportunity to break loose.
Zh: 她已经在这里躲了一些时日了，其间没人陪她说话聊天。让她接着说，你说不定能有机能摆脱困境。

En: That's illegal. I'm guessing it's patented... But we're beyond that, aren't we?
Zh: “你现在的行为已经触犯了法律。我猜机器应该是有专利的……但是现在发射的波段应该远超合法范围了吧？”

En: Oh yeah. *Way* beyond. She studies her death ray and the law officials trapped in it.
Zh: “是的，*远远*超过。”她观赏着自己的死亡电波以及身陷其中的执法人员。

En: Hzzzzzssssss... 342455797... Tere kallis... Hzzzzzzzssss... Palun ära ole ometi nii pilves, ometi nii pilves mu tüdruk... Hzzzzzzssss... 4464544829... hzzzssss... hzzzzssss...
Zh: 嗞嗞嗞嗞嗞嗞嗞……342455797……哦，亲爱的……嗞嗞嗞嗞嗞嗞嗞……请不要这么忧伤，依然如此忧伤，我的姑娘……嗞嗞嗞嗞嗞……4464544829……嗞嗞嗞嗞嗞……嗞嗞嗞嗞嗞……

En: Please, could you just turn it down... so I can... ask you something? (Move on.)
Zh: “求你了，能不能把机器稍微关小点……让我能……问你几句话？”（继续。）

En: *Please*, turn it off! I can't take it anymore! (Move on.)
Zh: “*求你*了，关了吧！我已经受不了了！”（继续。）

En: You're big boys, you can handle it. Stop being so damned melodramatic.
Zh: “你已经是大孩子了，你能承受得住，别演了。”

En: This is all... great, but let's talk... about the man who was killed.
Zh: “这真是……太棒了，不过我们还是聊聊……那个死掉的男人吧。”

En: Yeah, let's not talk about *that* shit. You were hunting me and fell into my trap instead. That's all there is to say about it.
Zh: “行啊，我们还是不要聊*那件*破事儿了。你追捕我了一路，结果却掉入我的陷阱。我说完了。”

En: So she thinks of you as hunters -- not cops -- and of herself merely as prey.
Zh: 所以她觉得你是个猎人——而非警察——而她自己不过是猎物而已。

En: Fine. I can dial it down. I've also got a gun, by the way. She steps reluctantly out of the shadows. The pain lessens.
Zh: “好吧。我可以把它调小。顺便提一嘴，我手上还有枪呢。”她不情愿地从阴影中走了出来。痛苦减轻了。

En: 236189281... If you're looking for a deal on mattresses... SUHSUHSUHSPEEDFRRRR... 23567... 32971047302819... O' strange light, O' light of mine..
Damn... this... The lieutenant clutches his head, grimacing.
Zh: 警督抓着自己的头，表情十分痛苦。“该死……这个……”

En: Goddamnit. She regards you and Kim with sudden sympathy.
Zh: “真他妈要命。”她突然略带同情地看着你和金。

En: Goddamnit. She regards you and Kim with sudden sympathy.
Zh: “真他妈要命。”她突然略带同情地看着你和金。

En: She's carrying a two-barrelled front-loader. Remain careful.
Zh: 她带着把双管前膛枪。千万要小心。

En: The pale? It's the end of the world.
Zh: “灰域？灰域不是在世界的尽头吗？”

En: When I came into town was there... anyone with me?
Zh: “我刚到瑞瓦肖的时候……有其他人跟我一起吗？”

En: One of the guys seemed chipper, a blond, the other had a brooding something-or-other about him... And the woman... the woman was the only one in uniform. All were carrying.
Zh: “有个看起来很爽朗的金发男，另外一个男的好像有心事……还有个女的……只有她穿了制服。他们仨都带着家伙。”

En: She narrows her eyes. "That sound about right?"
Zh: 她眯起眼睛。“听起来应该差不多吧？”

En: Fantastic. I've gotta get on the road -- then you can go find your friends... Unless you have anything *pressing* to ask me.
Zh: “那太好了。我得上路了——然后你能去找你的朋友了……除非你有现在什么*要紧事*要问我。”

En: Hmgh... she's right. No one mentioned *hearing*... shots. I... the shot didn't come from the roof.
Zh: “嗯……她没说错，确实没人提到说*听到*……枪声什么的。我……凶手不是在屋顶开的枪。”

En: Hold still and try to have an apocalyptic vision.
Zh: 静止不动，试着想象世界末日的景象。

En: Officer, are you okay?
Zh: “警官，你还好吗？”

En: Don't worry, Kim, I'm just trying to... have... an apocalyptic... vision...
Zh: “别担心，金，我只是在试着……想象……世界末日……的景象……”

En: The white noise turns into a wall of mist and grey mould, bubbling, sweeping over the city... it tears up buildings and raises sidewalks into the sky. It's Revachol -- at the end of the world.
Zh: 白噪音变成一堵迷雾和灰霉组成的沸腾高墙，笼罩了整座城市……撕裂了建筑，把人行布道刮到了半空中。这就是瑞瓦肖——世界末日的瑞瓦肖。

En: What happens when it *really* starts?
Zh: 如果*真的*发生了的话会怎么样？

En: What will happen to *me*? (Try to crawl back.)
Zh: *我*会怎么样？（试着爬回去。）

En: And then? The Unimaginable Event?
Zh: 那之后呢？会发生什么难以言喻的大事件吗？

En: What are you doing? I told you -- it's best not to move...
Zh: “你在做什么？我告诉过你了——最好不要动……”

En: The pain intensifies as your hands attempt to reach out into the pale chaos.
Zh: 你的手试着伸入灰域混沌中，随之而来的痛苦也逐渐加剧。

En: The visions compete for space in your brain with the hailstorm of radio signals coming from the compressor. You feel like your skull is about to shatter into smithereens.
Zh: 压缩机发射的无线电讯号像雹暴般袭来，不同的幻景在你的大脑中拼命地争夺着地盘。你感觉自己的头骨快要碎成齑粉了。

En: Fragments on bone scattering, like your thoughts at this moment, sticking now to forebodings, now to a strangely familiar voice...
Zh: 头骨碎片四散，有如你此刻的思想，此刻附着于不祥的预感之上，附着于一个出奇熟悉的声音之上……

En: Proximity to the Pale Compressor is overloading his mind. He'll be okay once it's off, though.
Zh: “过度接近灰域压缩机让他的大脑超载了。等机器关掉之后他就会没事的。”

En: Officer... what are you doing, you...
Zh: “警官……你在做什么，你……”

En: ...and it hasn't even really started yet.
Zh: ……而且它甚至还没真的开始呢。

En: Nothing. Just slow disappearance. You look at a tree or a coal shed, then look away, and, turning back...
Zh: 什么都没有。只有缓慢的消逝。你看见一棵树，或是一间煤矿小屋。你转开视线，又转了回来……

En: The same thing that happens to all humans. You will become vapour. Dissolve into frequencies. Then those frequencies will be forgotten.
Zh: 同样的事也会发生在所有人类身上。你会变成水蒸气，融化成频率。然后这些频率会被彻底遗忘。

En: Yes, but even in this anguish you can't see its precise shape. It's *unimaginable* for a reason.
Zh: 是的，但即使是在极度的痛苦中，你还是看不清它的准确形状。它*不可想象*是有原因的。

En: Yeah, we saw... 98713... hhhsssszzzz... completely lost his mind, or... hhhhhsssssszzzz... prenatal vitamins, because no child is a blank slate... 352628789... shameless asshole...
Zh: “是的，我们看见……98713……嗞嗞嗞嗞嗞嗞……完全失去理智了，或者是……嗞嗞嗞嗞嗞嗞……产前维生素，因为没有小孩是一块白板……352628789……无耻的混蛋……”

En: He didn't deign to stop to chat ... 98713... hhhsssszzzz... completely lost his mind, or... hhhhhsssssszzzz... prenatal vitamins, because no child is a blank slate... 352628789... shameless asshole...
Zh: “他都不屑于停下来聊个两句……98713……嗞嗞嗞嗞嗞嗞……完全失去理智了，或者是……嗞嗞嗞嗞嗞嗞……产前维生素，因为没有小孩是一块白板……352628789……无耻的混蛋……”

En: ...the shed will be gone, and you won't even remember that the tree was ever there, much less what kind of tree it was, a pine or a birch. You won't remember if a man had been hanging from it.
Zh: ……小屋会消失，你不会记得那里曾经有过一棵树，更别说树的品种是松还是桦了。你也不会记得，是不是有个男人就吊在那上面。

En: We arrested her... consider yourself avenged.
Zh: “我们逮捕了她……就当是为你报仇了吧。”

En: Say nothing.
Zh: 什么也不说。

En: It didn't help her. She was a... fugitive. We took her in.
Zh: “那只是徒劳。她毕竟是个……逃犯。我们逮捕了她。”

En: Oh, I wouldn't have wished that upon her... The girl was playing some dangerous games, but even with what she did to me... I wouldn't have wished *that* upon her.
Zh: “哦，我不希望她的结局会是这样……那个女孩是在玩火，但是即便她这么对我了之后……我也不希望她会落得*那种下场*……”

En: I'm sad to hear that. The girl was playing some dangerous games, but even with what she did to me... I wouldn't have wished *that* upon her.
Zh: “真是个坏消息……那个女孩是在玩火，但是即便她这么对我了之后……我也不希望她会落得*那种下场*……”

En: She frowns. "She had some pretty messed up people after her. She's going to come out of that meat grinder a different person... If she comes out at all."
Zh: 她皱起眉头。“有一群很危险的人在追着她。从大牢里出来之后，她会脱胎成一个完全不同的人……假设她还出得来的话。”

En: She'll age even faster in prison than she was partying. A pity.
Zh: 她进监狱之后衰老的速度会比她天天派对还要快。可惜了。

En: There was a longing in her for some sort of denouement, but it's unlikely that it was this... turning prematurely grey while being escorted from interrogation room to interrogation room...
Zh: 她的内心深处渴望着某种结局……但大概不是现在这种……从一个审讯室被押送到另一个审讯室，一夜白头……

En: But Klaasje couldn't have known I was here. How did you?
Zh: “但是卡拉洁不可能知道我躲在这里。你是怎么知道的？”

En: Kim! Kim! Make it stop!
Zh: “金！金！快让它停下来！”

En: Look, I know this isn't very fun for you, so let's wrap this up. You have a tribunal to attend.
Zh: “你看，我知道这对你来说一点也不好玩，所以就到此为止吧。还有一场审判等着你去参加呢。”

En: Suddenly, your entire body is paralysed. Aggressive white noise fills your skull -- a strange pain like you've never felt before. The static overcomes you.
Zh: 顷刻间，你全身麻痹了。白噪声充斥着你的大脑——一阵前所未有的奇怪的痛楚接踵而至。透过静电流声，你听见了一个女人的声音。

En: There was... half an hour missing. You went out...
Zh: “中间……有半个小时你不在，你那段时间出去……”

En: Like *fuck* I did. I was *in* all the time -- I went for a leak, ten, fifteen minutes max.
Zh: “我不在*你个头*。我一直都*在酒吧里*——我去了趟厕所，顶多去了十到十五分钟吧。”

En: That merc was probably shot from a distance with a military-grade rifle. Otherwise everyone at the Whirling would have heard the shot downstairs.
Zh: 射杀雇佣兵的子弹是从很远的地方飞过来的，而且用的是军用步枪。否则飞旋旅社楼下的所有人都会在听到枪声。

En: That... isn't... it...
What the fuck, man? She's everyone's type. She's *your* type. Are you sure you're not the one who was excited about the peephole?
Zh: “你在说什么鬼呢，哥们儿？她是所有人喜欢的类型吧，也是*你喜欢的*类型。你敢说自己私底下没有对那个偷窥孔感到兴奋吗？”

En: She's beginning to suspect you've broken into her lorry now.
Zh: 现在她开始怀疑你进过她的货車了。

En: Maybe twist the knife? Just in case. Make her more desperate.
Zh: 应许该在火上再加点油？不怕一万，就怕万一。哀莫大于心死，而人死亦次之。

En: You know who he is -- he fucked me over. Country boy my ass...
Zh: “你明知故问……他把我耍得团团转。乡村男孩个屁……”

En: Okay, great, you got into my lorry on your own. What now? You're going to arrest me for drug trafficking? A bitter smile.
Zh: “好吧，厉害厉害，你靠自己进了我的货車。现在怎么说？准备以贩毒的罪名逮捕我了吗？”她露出一丝苦涩的微笑。

En: Beneath it, she's relieved Tommy didn't betray her.
Zh: 在内心深处，她因汤米没有背叛她而感到些许宽慰。

En: Yeah, that figures. She shrugs. "What now? You're going to arrest me for drug trafficking?"
Zh: “意料之中。”她耸耸肩。“现在怎么说？准备以贩毒的罪名逮捕我了吗？”

En: She's more upset than she lets on.
Zh: 她比表面上更郁闷。

En: Man, it's to get *away* from all that murderous shit that I left the Madre for the Union.
Zh: “伙计，我离开马德雷加入工会为的就是*远离*那些杀人的事。”

En: Yeah? Where?
Zh: “是吗？哪里？”

En: Yeah? Where?
Zh: “是吗？哪里？”

En: More. More questions before doing anything.
Zh: 继续。在进行下一步之前，继续问问题。

En: Damn it! Destroy that thing already!
Zh: 该死！赶快摧毁那个东西！

En: I'm listening.
Zh: “你说。”

En: A doubt creeps over you. She sounds so sure of everything. So... not guilty.
Zh: 一丝怀疑感爬上你的心头。她听上去对一切都如此胸有成竹，如此……问心无愧。

En: Wrong. That kind of pussy is *never* replaceable.
Zh: 不对，她那种妞*绝对*无可替代。

En: Is that blood on the ground before you? Are your ears *bleeding*?
Zh: 你面前地上的是血吗？你是不是耳朵*出血*了？

En: Go ahead -- it's your body.
Zh: “你请便——反正折腾的是你自己的身体。”

En: Hngh...
And mine too, he thinks -- but keep on. This must be done.
Zh: 还有我的身体也搭进去了，他想道——但是继续吧。也没别的办法了。

En: Was that... pity? For you? She sees you're grasping for straws here.
Zh: 那是……怜悯吗？对你的怜悯？她看着你挣扎着想要抓住最后一根稻草。

En: I wish... I could use my gun right now.
Zh: “我真希望……现在手边有枪可用。”

En: You won't get to. Your dirty little tricks won't work with me, she responds, holding your gaze.
Zh: “你办不到。你那点肮脏的小把戏对我没有用的，”她凝视着你，回应道。

En: She didn't hate him, okay?
Zh: 她并不恨他，知道吗？

En: Hold on, *no one* takes a fifteen-minute leak.
Zh: “等一下，*谁*上厕所上十五分钟啊。”

En: Look, fuck you, man. I might have also stopped by the bar.
Zh: “你去死吧。我好像还去过吧台。”

En: She speaketh truth.
Zh: 此女所言非虚。

En: No one takes a... -- the lieutenant winces from the pain -- "fifteen-minute leak."
Zh: “*谁*上厕所，”警督因为疼痛而抽搐着，“上十五分钟啊。”

En: Look, fuck you, man. I might have also stopped by the bar.
Zh: “你去死吧。我好像还去过吧台。”

En: She speaketh truth.
Zh: 此女所言非虚。

En: Goddamnit... The lieutenant grabs his head.
Zh: “该死……”警督抓紧了自己的脑袋。

En: That didn't go super well. You've gotta lay something better on her.
Zh: 情况不乐观啊。你得找点更好的说辞。

En: A breechloader?
Zh: “一把后膛枪？”

En: No.
Zh: “没有。”

En: This is such a wipeout.
Zh: 真是全军覆没。

En: Are you a... girl-liker?
Zh: “你…….喜欢女孩吗吗？”

En: What the hell, man?! She laughs. "Yeah, why not? I've gotten worse."
Zh: “什么鬼？！”她笑了。“是啊，怎么不可以吗？我还有更糟糕的呢。”

En: Her hand slipped from the dial of the compressor for a moment, almost turning it down. Then she put her hand back -- not yet. Not yet...
Zh: 她的手在压缩机的转盘上逗留了片刻，好像要把它调小，然后又把手收了回去——还没到时候。没到时候……

En: Actually... (grotesquely whine from the pain) "I did get pretty excited..."
Zh: “其实……”（因为痛苦发出诡异的呜咽声）“我都快高潮了……”

En: Cool, man, cool. I'm glad you got off on it --  what the *fuck* are we talking about?
Zh: “很好，哥们儿，非常好。很高兴你没把这事儿放在心上——我们*他妈的*到底在聊什么？”

En: Right on.
Zh: 好极了。

En: But I do want... details, actually... (Cringe from the head-splitting torment.)
Zh: “但我真的想听你……说说细节……”（因为剧烈的头痛蜷成一团。）

En: Seriously, just move on.
Zh: 说真的，还是继续吧。

En: ...Really?
Zh: “……你认真的吗？”

En: Yeah, *really*?
Zh: “是啊，*你认真的吗*？”

En: Okay... then what happened?
Zh: “好吧……然后呢？”

En: No ill will there. It wasn't a problem for her.
Zh: 没有敌意。她应该不会介意。

En: I wish I... had my gun right now...
Zh: “真希望我的配枪……现在还在我手里……”

En: Not for a moment do I believe that *you* came here unarmed.
Zh: “我才不信*你这样的人*会手无寸铁的来到这里。”

En: She's not letting her guard down.
Zh: 她没有放下自己的防备。

En: That really comes as a blow to her.
Zh: 这对她来说真是当头一棒。

En: Who's Tommy?
Zh: “汤米是谁？”

En: No, he didn't. I found my own way in. (Lie.)
Zh: “跟他没关系，我自己开的車门。”（撒谎。）

En: No, he didn't. I found my own way in.
Zh: “跟他没关系，我自己开的車门。”

En: Yeah, I made him talk.
Zh: “是啊，我让他开了口的。”

En: Yeah, I made him talk. (Lie.)
Zh: “是啊，我让他开了口的。”（撒谎。）

En: She deliberately avoided naming the mob she worked for. You might be able to find this out later.
Zh: 她有意没说出自己老板的名字。也许之后你有机会能查出来。

En: There's a sinister note in her voice. Even with the gun and the compressor, she's *afraid* of you.
Zh: 她的声音里透露着杀气。就算有了枪和压缩机傍身，她还是*怕*你。

En: There's a sinister note in her voice. Even with the gun and the compressor, she's afraid of you.
Zh: 她的声音里透露着杀气。就算有了枪和压缩机傍身，她还是怕你。

En: Hnghh... The lieutenant is unable to articulate his question.
Zh: “唔……”警督没办法组织好他的问题。

En: There. It's going to be easier to reach the machine now.
Zh: 好了。现在要够到机器更容易了。

En: Sure don't.
Zh: “当然没有。”

En: I hate to be the bearer of bad tidings, but I don't think she's perjuring herself.
Zh: 吾辈虽不愿作逆耳之言，但仍觉得此人所言非虚。

En: I can't quite tell... what *kind* of gun is it?
Zh: “我也分辨不出来……那是什么*类型*的枪？”

En: Yeah, sure, and I didn't like Wild Pines sending in those foreign hirelings -- me and a fuck-ton of other people around here.
Zh: “是啊，当然，而且我也不喜欢野松公司派驻外国佣兵的行为……我以及附近一大票人都是这个立场。”

En: Did you feel... protective of the Union?
Zh: “你……站在工会这一边吗？”

En: Look, I'm not aware of any hole in Klaasje's wall. And if I had been, I would have told her to get it sealed -- that's what friends do.
Zh: “听着，我根本不知道卡拉洁房间的墙上有洞。而且就算我知道，我也会让她封起来——朋友都会这么做。”

En: And... despite everything, you helped her by staging the lynching?
Zh: “而且……抛开所有这一切，你还是帮她布置了私刑现场？”

En: You think I was envious of his conquests? Look, pussy's not a problem for me. And definitely not a reason to off someone.
Zh: “你以为我妒忌他猎艳的战果吗？听着，妞对我来说才来都不是问题，更不可能成为我与某人交恶的理由。”

En: There's nothing more... personal that you had against him? Perhaps his... way with women?
Zh: “你对他……没什么私人恩怨吧？比如他……对待女人的方式？”

En: Is that the only reason you... hung out on the roof?
Zh: “这就是你……在屋顶逗留的全部理由？”

En: What's... so great about her antenna?
Zh: “她的天线……有什么厉害之处吗？”

En: Yes, I'm sure. And, anyway, as I said before, the shot had to have come from afar.
Zh: “是的，我确定。而且，我之前也说过了，子弹一定是从很远的地方射进来的。”

En: A Nachtwey 80 front-loader. Two-barrelled -- not really what you were looking for, I'm guessing.
Zh: “我这支是纳克特威80前膛枪，双管的——不是你在找的那一款，我猜对了吧。”

En: There's a... hole in the wall. You can see into Klaasje's room... I wouldn't blame you if you've..
Zh: “墙上……有个洞，从里面可以看到卡拉洁的房间……我不会怪你的，如果你……”

En: So you *haven't* been.. watching Klaasje through a hole in.. wall?
Zh: “所以你*从来没有*……通过墙上的洞……偷窥过卡拉洁？”

En: Klaasje said... you may have used the *peephole* in her wall to spy on her...
Zh: “卡拉洁说……你可能通过墙上的*偷窥孔*监视过她……”

En: Yeah. The girl seemed terrified. The merc was beyond caring what happened to his mortal coil. It was a no-brainer.
Zh: “是啊。那姑娘好像吓坏了。佣兵已经没办法再去关心尘世间的纷纷扰扰了，显而易见。”

En: What did you think? That I'm going to squeal on my gun supplier? Sorry, I'm not that kind of gal.
Zh: “你想什么呢？你觉得我会供出自己的枪支供应商？抱歉，我不是那种人。”

En: That doesn't... sound like the name of a real store.
Zh: “听起来……不像是真正商店的名字。”

En: Really? Trigger...
Zh: “真的吗？好战……”

En: Trigger-Happy Jack's.
Zh: “好战杰克。”

En: Damn. That girl's pretty, but not *that* pretty. In fact, no one's pretty enough to waste time skulking about the Whirling, drilling holes in walls...
Zh: “妈的。那个姑娘的确很好看，但也没*那么*好看。事实上，这世上没人能好看到值得让人浪费时间潜伏在飞旋旅社，还在墙上打洞的地步……”

En: What gun store?
Zh: “哪家枪店？”

En: The gun store.
Zh: “枪店。”

En: No, they're not practical. Break too often.
Zh: “没有，古董枪不好用，很容易坏。”

En: Never really got into weapons trafficking. Especially not old weapons -- they break too often, and you don't want unhappy customers among gun collectors.
Zh: “我从没走私过武器，特别是旧武器——它们太容易坏了，没有商家会想要拥有不满意的顾客。”

En: If I want to stare at a pretty girl, I can pick one up at a bar -- or, worst case scenario, look at a naughty photo. Who has time to go skulking about the Whirling, drilling *holes* in walls?
Zh: “如果我想看漂亮姑娘，我可以在酒吧直接找一个——或者，再不济就看点小黄图呗，谁那么闲会潜伏在飞旋旅社，还要在墙上打个洞？”

En: Do you... collect guns... maybe old rifles?
Zh: “你有没有……收集过枪支……比如说旧步枪？”

En: I know you don't like old things, but do you... sell antique rifles on the side?
Zh: “我知道你不喜欢老旧的东西，但是你有没有……顺手倒卖些古董步枪什么的？”

En: I see it's a front-loader. Do you have another... gun somewhere?
Zh: “这是把前膛枪。你还有……其他的枪吗？”

En: Where did you get it?
Zh: “这把枪你是从哪里弄来的？”

En: So you're... *sure* you didn't shoot the merc... from the roof? (Move on.)
Zh: “所以你……*确定*自己没有在屋顶上……朝雇佣兵开枪吗？”（继续。）

En: You have a gun.
Zh: “你有一把枪。”

En: Klaasje looks like... a girl in one of your posters. She seems to be *your type*.
Zh: “卡拉洁看起来……很像你的某张海报上的女孩。她好像是*你喜欢的那种类型*。”

En: You... liked Klaasje *a lot*.
Zh: “你……*尤其*中意卡拉洁。”

En: Plenty of broken people who don't come with that kind of body count. Besides, they're paid well for what they do.
Zh: “很多哪怕已经无可救药的败类也不会有他们手下那么多条人命。再说了，他们做的那一切都是有丰厚酬劳的。”

En: My type? She's everyone's type. She's *your* type. Are you sure you're not the one who was excited about the peephole?
Zh: “我喜欢的类型？她是所有人喜欢的类型吧，也是*你喜欢的*类型。你敢说自己私底下没有对那个偷窥孔感到兴奋吗？”

En: I have other questions. (Conclude.)
Zh: “我还有别的问题。”（总结。）

En: You don't feel... sympathy for mercs? It's hard work.
Zh: “你对佣兵……没有半点同情？那可是门苦差事。”

En: Would you say that Lely was... a *likeable* person?
Zh: “你会觉得那个莱利是个……*讨人喜欢*的人吗？”

En: Play... pinball much?
Zh: “你经常……玩弹球吗？”

En: I didn't like him. Hardened mercenaries aren't particularly likeable types.
Zh: “我不喜欢他。久经沙场的佣兵算不上是讨喜的那类人。”

En: See her confident gaze, her toned arms -- yeah, she wouldn't have had much trouble in the intimacy department.
Zh: 看着她自信的眼神，结实的手臂——是啊，她在亲密关系方面不会遇到什么麻烦。

En: No, not since I was 14 and hanging out at the only diner in Dardanne. Haven't been into low-risk, no-reward games since moving to the city.
Zh: “玩得不多。我14岁那年在达丹内唯一的餐厅游手好闲之后就没再玩过。自打我搬进城里之后就对这种低风险、无奖励的游戏没了兴趣。”

En: There are some... mysterious pinball machines in some pretty... mysterious rooms in the Whirling...
Zh: “有几台……神神秘秘的弹球游戏机……就放在飞旋旅社的神神秘秘的房间里……”

En: Is peeping... one of your hobbies?
Zh: “你有……偷窥的爱好吗？”

En: Wow. Pissing, pinball, and now peeping. I'm just not following your insinuations, detective.
Zh: “哇哦。尿尿，弹球，现在又成偷窥了。我真的跟不上你的暗示，警探。”

En: I'm not following your clever insinuations, detective.
Zh: “我听不懂你在暗示些什么，警探。”

En: Okay, let's take a... step back. (Conclude.)
Zh: “好吧，让我们……说回刚才的。”（总结。）

En: Do you like to... hang out on rooftops?
Zh: “你喜欢……在屋顶待着吗？”

En: Yeah, and probably some ghosts from the time of the Suzerain. I'm not really interested in supranatural mysteries.
Zh: “硬要说的话可能是宗主国时期的幽灵吧。我对超自然话题真的不怎么感兴趣。”

En: What about... elevators? Do you like *elevators*?
Zh: “那……电梯呢？你喜欢*电梯*吗？”

En: The fuck do elevators have to do with anything?
Zh: “他妈的电梯跟这些有什么关系吗？”

En: Nope. Look, she has an *effect* on people. That je ne sais quoi that makes it impossible not to look at her when she walks into the room -- and very difficult to look away. But -- travel enough, and you realize...
Zh: “没有。听着，她可以对其他人产生*影响*。当她步入一个房间的时候，她身上有种难以描述的东西让人没办法不看她——而且之后目光就很难再转开。不过——如果看得多了，你就会意识到……”

En: Wait... (wince from the pain) "what kinds of mysteries interest you, then?"
Zh: “等等，”（因为疼痛而抽搐），“那你会对什么未解之谜感兴趣？”

En: Not murder mysteries, either, if that's what you're thinking.
Zh: “也不是什么谋杀之谜，如果你在想这个的话。”

En: Do you or don't you?!
Zh: “所以你到底是喜欢还是不喜欢？！”

En: Not particularly.
Zh: “没什么特别感兴趣的。”

En: Why not?
Zh: “为什么不可以呢？”

En: Yes, I'm sure. She pauses. "In fact, the shot couldn't have possibly come from the roof -- otherwise we would have heard it downstairs."
Zh: “是的，我确定。”她停顿了一下。“事实上，开枪的地点不可能是屋顶——不然我们在楼下肯定会听到。”

En: Not even... (grab your head) "quaint old rickety ones?"
Zh: “就算是……”（抓住你的头），“那些古怪的东倒西歪的东西也不感兴趣吗？”

En: Why are *you* so hung up on elevators?
Zh: “*你*为什么老是揪着电梯的问题不放？”

En: That's a pretty good question, actually. She clearly doesn't know anything about the secret passage.
Zh: 其实，这是个很不错的问题。她显然完全不知道那条秘密通道的事。

En: Okay then... I have other questions for you.
Zh: “那好吧……我还有别的问题要问你。”

En: I'm not really into old shit for old shit's sake.
Zh: “我对那些古老的破事没半点兴趣。”

En: And?
Zh: “所以呢？”

En: Hmh... No one mentioned *hearing* any shots. And a rifle like the one used would have been *loud*. She's right... I don't think the shot came from... the roof.
Zh: “嗯……没人提过*听见*……枪声的事。凶手使用的那种步枪开枪的声音应该是*很大*的。她说得有道理，我也觉得开枪的地点不在房顶。”

En: And that topic was what? Staircases? Ladders?
Zh: “我们之前说什么正经话题了吗？楼梯？梯子？”

En: She looks you in the eye impassively, making it clear that she's not planning to comment on the matter any further.
Zh: 她冷漠地看着你的眼睛，显然她不打算对此再作出任何的评论。

En: So you know she's not an antiques enthusiast. Not my jurisdiction, man, but it also doesn't sound like she used that secret route. At all.
Zh: 所以你现在知道了，她没有收集古董的爱好。虽说这不是我的管辖范围，老兄，不过她好像也没有用过那条秘密通道。完全没有用过。

En: It's just, the hole in Klaasje's wall is.. pretty suspicious...
Zh: “只是，卡拉洁房间墙上的洞……太可疑了……”

En: It's very powerful. I used it to tune into RCM frequencies. That's how I knew to be prepared for your arrival.
Zh: “这台机器很强力。我用它接收RCM的频道，因此我才知道要准备迎接你们的到来。”

En: Who doesn't? Oh, you probably mean Klaasje's rooftop. Sure, I've hung out there. She's got this great antenna...
Zh: “谁不喜欢屋顶呢？哦，你是说卡拉洁的屋顶。当然了，我喜欢待在那儿。她有一根很棒的天线……”

En: The view's pretty bomb too, but you might say the antenna was the main attraction there, yeah. Along with Klaasje.
Zh: “景色也挺美的，不过你也可以说天线是最有吸引力的。卡拉洁也是。”

En: (Wince.) "There's other evidence I want to ask you about." (Conclude.)
Zh: （抽搐。）“我还有其他证据想问你。”（总结。）

En: Yeah. *Evidence*.
Zh: “好啊。*证据*。”

En: That's a shame... because I happen to be a para-natural detective.
Zh: “真遗憾……我可是个超能力警探呢。”

En: Cool. They sent a ghost-cop after me. Congratulations.
Zh: “好赞哦，他们派了个通灵警探来抓我。还真是可喜可贺。”

En: I considered her a good friend, yeah.
Zh: “我把她当成一个好朋友，没错。”

En: But you wanted to be more than just *friends*.
Zh: “但是你可不止是想跟她做*朋友*。”

En: She said you wanted to run... away with her.
Zh: “她说你想……跟她一起私奔。”

En: There are... other things I... ask about. (Conclude.)
Zh: “还有些……其他事情我……想问你。”（总结。）

En: Oh, so that's where you were going with this. She shakes her head. "Do I *really* seem like someone who would do something as lame as committing murder out of jealousy?"
Zh: “哦，原来你想说的就是这个。”她摇摇头。“我*真的*看起来像是那种会因为妒忌而杀人的挫人吗？”

En: That's a very sentimental way of putting it. We both had *pasts* we didn't want to catch up with us. And we enjoyed listening to music together. Why not go on a road trip?
Zh: “这是种煽情的说法。我俩都有想要抛弃的*过去*，也喜欢一起听音乐。一起上路又有何不可呢？”

En: Did you leave any... flowers for Klaasje on the roof?
Zh: “你有没有……在屋顶给卡拉洁留过花？”

En: No, gifts of flowers and candy aren't really my style.
Zh: “没有，送人花和糖真心不是我的风格。”

En: Not even wildflowers? May bell... for example?
Zh: “连野花都没有吗？比如说……铃兰？”

En: These weren't just flowers -- they... symbols... of... Revolution. (Push through the pain.)
Zh: “铃兰不仅仅是花——它们……是大革命的……象征。”（硬扛过疼痛。）

En: But Klaasje was... mourning...
Zh: “但是卡拉洁……在哀悼……”

En: I never did understand why, when someone dies, a hothouse's worth of flowers has to die too.
Zh: “我永远都理解不了，为什么每次一有人死了，温室里的花也得跟着凋谢。”

En: She raises her eyebrows. "Nope."
Zh: 她抬高眉毛。“没有。”

En: So now I'm leaving Revolutionary symbols around... Come on.
Zh: “所以我现在又开始在到处留下大革命的符号了……拜托。”

En: Okay, so you... didn't leave May bells... (Conclude.)
Zh: “好吧，所以铃兰花……不是你留的……”（总结。）

En: No, I did not.
Zh: “不是我。”

En: Do you have an... alibi? For when Lely was shot?
Zh: “你有没有……不在场证明。就是莱利中枪的时候？”

En: Man, I was with the boys the whole night. I hope they at least bothered to impress *that* upon you.
Zh: “兄弟，我那天一整晚都跟那些小伙子们在一起。希望他们至少能费心跟你提过*这一点*。”

En: They did say you left to take a... really long leak. Fifteen minutes.
Zh: “他们提到你去过厕所……而且去了很久。十五分钟。”

En: There were... ten minutes they couldn't account for.
Zh: “至少……有十分钟的时间你不在他们视线里。”

En: You mean, the length of a toilet break? That wouldn't even have been enough time.
Zh: “你是说，我上厕所的那段时间？那点时间根本不够去杀个人。”

En: Yeah, and I'm sure they also made some FUNNY remarks about it -- they always do. I've driven a lot of long haul and chugged a lot of beer, man.
Zh: “是啊，而且我敢肯定他们还对此说了些俏皮话吧——他们总是这样。兄弟，我那天开了很长时间的車，喝了很多啤酒。”

En: She scoffs. "Can't do either without some power of mind over bladder. And, anyway, that wouldn't have been enough time."
Zh: 她自嘲道。“要是没有能够压倒膀胱的意志力，应该是做不到的。再说了，那点时间根本不够去杀个人。”

En: Our investigation... (wince from the pain) "has shown that fifteen minutes was *just* enough time to commit the murder."
