Zh: “根据我们的调查……（因为疼痛而抽搐）显示，那十五分钟*刚好*足够实施谋杀。”

En: Wow, now I'm curious. Please, explain.
Zh: “哇哦，我还挺感兴趣的。愿闻其详。”

En: Why?
Zh: “为什么？”

En: Somewhere on the outskirts of Faubourg, a scrawny teenage girl stands by the side of the road, greeting passing vehicles with a calloused thumb turned upward. She's tired. She wants to leave. But she knows she will come back.
Zh: 在郊区的某个地方，一个骨瘦如柴的少女站在路旁，竖起长着老茧的拇指招呼着来往的车辆。她很疲惫。她想离开。但她知道自己还会回来的。

En: Look, there's a secret way from the ground floor of the Whirling to the roof.
Zh: “是这样的，飞旋旅社一楼有条秘密通道，可以通往屋顶。”

En: Okay... never mind mysteries. There's a... secret way from the Whirling bar to the roof.
Zh: “好吧……神秘点也没关系。有一条……秘密通道，可以从飞旋旅社的吧台通往屋顶。”

En: Never mind the... pinball then. There's a... secret way from the Whirling bar... to the roof.
Zh: “那就没关系了……弹球的事。有一条……秘密通道，可以从飞旋旅社的吧台……通往屋顶。”

En: Look, there's a... secret way from the first floor of the Whirling to the roof.
Zh: “是这样的，飞旋旅社一楼……有一条通往屋顶的秘密通道。”

En: I think we may have gone a little off-topic.
Zh: “我觉得我们好像有点跑题了。”

En: Don't know it. But also... She frowns, studying your face.
Zh: “不知道。不过……”她皱起眉头，打量着你的脸。

En: Evaluating your competence as a police officer.
Zh: 她正在评估你作为一名警察的能力。

En: ...the shot couldn't have come from the roof. Or we would have all heard it downstairs.
Zh: “……开枪的地点绝不是屋顶，否则我们在楼下全都能听见。”

En: She has a... point there. No one mentioned... The pain stops him from finishing the sentence.
Zh: “她说的……有道理。没人提过……”疼痛打断了他的话。

En: I'm listening.
Zh: “我在听。”

En: Klaasje said you got very... angry when she started seeing Lely -- threatened her.
Zh: “卡拉洁说当她刚开始跟莱利约会的时候，你变得非常……暴躁——还威胁她。”

En: She rejected me with some wishy-washy bullshit about how she was confused because she felt so close to me and valued my friendship so much, and how guilty she felt for leading me on...
Zh: “她用一些片汤话拒绝了我，跟我说她很困惑，因为她感觉和我很亲近，也珍视我们之间的友谊，而且因为引诱我感到内疚……”

En: I knew that wasn't the whole story, but thought, fine, I'll take it and move on.
Zh: “我知道这不是事实的全部，不过无所谓，我接受了，然后翻篇。”

En: Yeah, one time when we'd both been drinking, I said some heated things about how dangerous her patterns with men were.
Zh: “是啊，有一次我们都喝多了，我说了些气话，说她跟男人的相处模式很危险。”

En: I was a little worried she'd blow it out of proportion in her head -- all the drugs she was doing can make you feel like you're living in a Dolorian tragedy.
Zh: “我有点担心，她大脑有些失调——她嗑的那些药足以让人觉得自己生活在德洛莉丝时代的悲剧中。”

En: She touched that radiant beauty, that silver wellspring of desire?
Zh: 她触碰到了那美丽的光点，那银色的欲望源泉？

En: Klaasje only said they "maybe kissed". Someone is lying here.
Zh: 卡拉洁只说她们*也许亲吻过*。有人在撒谎。

En: Wait -- Klaasje said you only kissed...
Zh: “等等——卡拉洁说你们只是接过吻……”

En: Hold on, Klaasje only... likes truly buff men!
Zh: “等一下，卡拉洁……只喜欢真正强壮的男人！”

En: Then what happened?
Zh: “然后发生什么了？”

En: She rolls her eyes again. "If that's what she wants on the record, so be it. I'm not about to go into details for you to jerk off to later."
Zh: 她又翻了翻白眼。“如果她是这么说的，那就这样吧。我不会再一一详述细节供你们待会儿回去意淫。”

En: She does know how to pick 'em.
Zh: “她确实知道该怎么做。”

En: Okay, fine. She rolls her eyes. "I was into her. Klaasje was into me too, for a time, I know it -- we even fooled around once. And, yeah, after that I thought maybe we could make a go of it."
Zh: “好吧。”她翻了翻白眼。“我喜欢她，她也喜欢我，我们互相喜欢了那么一段时间，我知道的——我们甚至还上过一次床。没错，在那次之后我觉得也许我们俩能成。”

En: By the way! Dolorian tragedies are still held in high regard for innovation in language and motifs. They are also know for overwrought emotions leading to catastrophic outcomes.
Zh: 对了！德洛莉丝时代的悲剧仍因其高度重视语言和主旨的创新而饱受赞誉，还以过激的情感而引发灾难性的后果而闻名。

En: You're running... drugs for the Union. I've been through your lorry.
Zh: “你在……为工会贩毒。我查过你的货車了。”

En: So, heart-of-gold Tommy fucked me over too... She shakes her head, slowly. "Never trust a musician."
Zh: 她慢慢摇摇头。“所以，善良如汤米也辜负我了……永远也不要相信一个音乐家。”

En: You're a... criminal. I can't trust anything you say.
Zh: “你是个……罪犯。你说的话我不能照单全收。”

En: This means you're... already a known killer. Because *drugs* kill people!
Zh: “这就意味着……你已经确定是个杀人犯了，因为*毒品*能杀人！”

En: You had... a financial incentive to kill the merc. (Move on.)
Zh: “你有……杀害雇佣兵的经济动机。”（继续。）

En: She scoffs. "That's your prerogative."
Zh: 她嗤笑道。“那是你的特权。”

En: Drugs don't make people abuse them; hopelessness does. As for the drug trade -- it would continue with or without me. I like to think that my efficiency as an organizer has at least prevented some internal conflicts.
Zh: “让人滥用毒品的不是毒品本身，而是绝望。至于贩毒活动——有我没我照样会继续下去。我更愿意这么想：作为一个组织者，至少我的高效避免了一些内部冲突的发生。”

En: Man, it's to get *away* from all that murderous shit that I left Jamrock -- my previous employer -- for the Union.
Zh: “老兄，我之所以*离开*加姆洛克——以及我的前雇主——并加入工会——为的就是离杀人越货之类的勾当远一点。”

En: I got lucky being a dispatcher -- never had to do any of the *really* dirty work myself. This gun, she glances at it, "has only been used for self-defence against serious scum." She turns the knob down just a millimetre.
Zh: 她把旋钮稍微调小了一毫米，然后继续说道：“分配到调度员的活儿其实挺走运的——你永远也不用亲手做些*真正*的脏活。这把枪，”她瞥了一眼，“只是为了在面对真正的人渣的时候进行正当防卫。”

En: But... you're threatening us with it!
Zh: “但是……你现在却在拿这把枪威胁我们！”

En: Based on what I've heard about you -- you *are* serious scum, she responds, holding your gaze.
Zh: “基于我听说过的你的传闻——你*就是*真正的人渣，”她回应道，没有避开你的目光。

En: She's proud of her organizational abilities even now -- and trying to raise that pride as a bulwark against the desperation.
Zh: 就算到了现在，她还是对自己的组织能力感到很骄傲——而且想要把这种自豪感作为对抗绝望的壁垒。

En: Nope. Look, she has an *effect* on people -- or had, before you sent her off in your Moralintern meat grinder. It's impossible not to look at her when she walks into the room -- and very difficult to look away. But -- travel enough, and you realize...
Zh: “没有。听着，她可以对其他人产生*影响*——或者说曾经可以产生影响……在你还没把她送进国际道德伦理委员会的绞肉机之前……当她步入一个房间的时候，她身上有种难以描述的东西让人没办法不看她——而且之后目光就很难再转开。不过——如果看得多了，你就会意识到……”

En: ...for the same reason that she's everyone's type as an object of desire, she's not irreplaceable.
Zh: “……所以她才是所有人欲望的对象，她并非是无可替代的。”

En: Oh, so that's where you were going with this.
Zh: “哦，所以你想说的是这个。”

En: The lieutenant watches her expectantly, occasionally shaking from the pain.
Zh: 警督充满期待地看着她，偶尔因为疼痛哆嗦一下。

En: Magazines? You should look through them.
Zh: 杂志？你应该翻翻看。

En: We should read this *immediately*. Like -- right now.
Zh: “我们应该*立马*查看一下。比如说——现在。”

En: Shine your flashlight on the books.
Zh: 用手电筒照亮那些书。

En: Look at the books.
Zh: 看看那些书。

En: Back out of the tent.
Zh: 离开帐篷。

En: Assorted softcovers, mostly pulp horror. A motor carriage lies buried in the snow on one cover, on another -- a ghost airship. You also see a collection of radio enthusiast magazines.
Zh: 各种各样的平装书，大部分都是恐怖小说。其中一本的封面上是一辆掩埋在雪中的汽車，还有一本——是一艘幽灵飞艇。你还发现一系列无线电爱好者的杂志。

En: Sift through the magazines.
Zh: 翻阅杂志。

En: 'Rega Monthly', 'Journal of Material Science'. More technological digest. One of the magazines doesn't have images on the cover -- it's not a magazine, it's a leather notebook!
Zh: 《每月音响》，《材料科学》，还有别的技术文摘。其中一本的封面没有图——那不是杂志，而是个皮革笔记本！

En: See anything? The lieutenant peeks in over your shoulder.
Zh: 警督从你身后看了一眼。“发现什么了吗？”

En: A notebook. (Take it.)
Zh: “一本笔记本。”（拿走。）

En: Look inside.
Zh: 看看里面。

En: Shine your flashlight on the books and magazines.
Zh: 用手电筒照亮那些书和杂志。

En: Sift through the books and magazines.
Zh: 翻阅书籍和杂志。

En: There she lies, cocooned in the sleeping bag, surrounded by empty cigarette packs, books, and half-read magazines.
Zh: 她就躺在那里，被睡袋紧紧包裹着，周围堆满了空的香烟盒、书本、还有翻了一半的杂志。

En: You see a rolled-up sleeping bag and personal belongings.
Zh: 你看见卷起来的睡袋，还有些私人物品。

En: We should put her in the sleeping bag so the rats don't get to her.
Zh: “我们应该把她放进睡袋，这样老鼠就没法咬她了。”

En: You'll need the lieutenant's help to carry her over.
Zh: 你需要警督的帮忙才能把她抬过来。

En: The plain red tent rests stoically in the corner. Ruby's dead body is wrapped in her sleeping bag.
Zh: 纯红色的帐篷稳稳地站在角落。露比的尸体就裹在她自己的睡袋里。

En: The plain red tent rests stoically in the corner.
Zh: 纯红色的帐篷稳稳地站在角落。

En: It reeks of cigarettes.
Zh: 它散发着一股香烟的味道。

En: The plain red tent stands by dispassionately.
Zh: 纯红色的帐篷平静地站立着。

En: It was pitched by practised hands -- she was used to camping out.
Zh: 这是一个老手扎的营——她习惯在外面露营了。

En: Look inside.
Zh: 看看里面。

En: Okay, let's carry her over here.
Zh: “好，我们把她抬到这里来吧。”

En: The lieutenant nods.
Zh: 警督点点头。

En: The tent looks old but well-maintained. In the darkness of the tent -- a rolled-up sleeping bag, cooking utensils, some books and a kerosene lamp.
Zh: 帐篷看起来很破旧，但维护的很好。黑漆漆的帐篷里面——有一只卷起来的睡袋，一些厨房用具，几本书，还有一只煤油灯。

En: Take the notebook.
Zh: 拿起笔记本。

En: It was pitched by practised hands -- she's used to camping out.
Zh: 这是一个老手扎的营——她习惯在外面露营了。

En: In the darkness of the tent: Ruby's body, zipped up in her sleeping bag. Next to the body -- some books and a kerosene lamp.
Zh: 黑漆漆的帐篷里面：露比的尸体包在睡袋里。旁边是几本书，还有一只煤油灯。

En: [Leave.]
Zh: [离开。]

En: The instigator's body has been removed by Processing. The tent seems orphaned now.
Zh: 教唆者的尸体被分析部的人移走了。现在这座帐篷看起来像个孤儿一样。

En: The plain red tent rests stoically in the corner.
Zh: 朴素的红色帐篷稳稳地站在角落。

En: You pocket the worn brown leather journal.
Zh: 你把那本磨损的皮质日记放进了自己的口袋。

En: A trusted friend left behind...
Zh: 一个值得信赖的朋友被留在了这里……

En: She watches by, motionless.
Zh: 她就这么看着，一动也不动。

En: I see the processing guys have been faster than usual. It's good we stopped by to check regardless. He nods at the lack of Ruby in the tent, then looks around. "Now -- island time."
Zh: “分析部的人的动作好像比平时要快。不管怎么说，我们路过来看一眼总是好的。”他朝着那个少了露比的帐篷点了点头，然后环顾了一下周围。“现在——该上岛了。”

En: Is everything all right in there, officer? Could you let me in?
Zh: “里面一切都还好吧，警官？我能进去吗？”

En: Could it be connected to the Mazov bust we found in the student's room?
Zh: “这个地方会不会跟我们之前在那个学生房间里发现的马佐夫半身像有关？”

En: Millions of depictions of Mazov have been produced. They're not all connected -- besides, that looked like some student. The youths always go for this kind of stuff...
Zh: “在这个世界上有上百万种马佐夫的画像或者塑像，这些艺术作品之间并不总是存在关联的……再说了，那个房间看起来像是间学生的宿舍，年轻人就喜欢这类符号……”

En: A long time ago? How long?
Zh: “很久以前是多久以前？”

En: Half a century? This was probably part of the network of defence posts the communards built against the amphibious landing. He looks around.
Zh: “约摸半个世纪前？这个地方可能是公社建造的防御工事网络的一部分，用于抵御两栖登陆作战。”

En: I think the purpose of this bunker was to produce propaganda. It would have had radio equipment back then, but that's all been looted.
Zh: 他看了看周围。“我觉得这个碉堡以前应该是个宣传站。那时候这里应该配备了无线电设备，不过后来都被洗劫一空了。”

En: A good hiding place for someone who's up to no good.
Zh: 对于恶人来说，这是个不错的藏身之处。

En: Could someone have stopped through here recently? (Conclude.)
Zh: “会不会有人最近来过这里？”（总结。）

En: You mean, like Ruby? No. I think we've stumbled on a piece of history. He looks at the dust.
Zh: 他看着浮尘。“你是指露比？应该没有。我觉得我们无意间发现了一块历史的残片。”

En: A moustachioed and mutton-chopped man, amateurishly depicted, gazes at you with sad eyes. A plaque reads: "K. Mazov." There is a spider web in the lower left corner of the portrait.
Zh: 这幅画的笔触略显生疏，画面上是一个蓄着大把络腮胡子的男人，他正悲伤地注视着你。画框上写着：“K·马佐夫。”肖像画左下方的角落里结着一张蜘蛛网。

En: Kras Mazov (nom de guerre), was an economist and a theoretical historian. He was a leading figure in the Graad side of the Turn-of-the-Century Revolution, where he headed the Eleven Day Government. Mazov is considered the father of scientific communism (Mazovian thought or Mazovianism).
Zh: 卡拉斯·马佐夫（假名），是一位经济学家以及理论历史学家。在统领了格拉德的那场世纪之交的大革命，领导了十一日政府。马佐夫被认为是科学康米主义之父（马佐夫思想或马佐夫主义）。

En: Who is K. Mazov?
Zh: “K·马佐夫是谁？”

En: Look, Kim! It's Comrade Mazov!
Zh: “看呐，金！是马佐夫同志！”

En: It's Kras Mazov, the idol of murdering traitors.
Zh: “卡拉斯·马佐夫，那些杀人成性的叛国者的偶像。”

En: Let's go. [Leave.]
Zh: “我们走吧。”[离开。]

En: [Leave.]
Zh: [离开。]

En: It's Kras Mazov! What a troublemaker he was.
Zh: “是卡拉斯·马佐夫。他可真是个惹事精。”

En: It's that Mazov guy. This must have been the hiding place of some naive leftists.
Zh: “是那个叫马佐夫什么的。这个地方肯定是那些天真的左翼分子以前的藏身地。”

En: Well, my amnesiac friend, Mazov is the father of scientific communism. His theories about economic history greatly influenced -- some would even say *sparked* -- the Antecentennial Revolution.
Zh: “好吧，我失忆的朋友，马佐夫是科学康米主义之父。他的经济历史理论影响了——有些人甚至可以说是*点燃*了——世纪初大革命。”

En: So my comrades were here.
Zh: “这么说来我的同志们以前来过这个地方。”

En: So... this must have been the hiding place of some antitraditionalist murderers?
Zh: “所以……这里一定就是那些反传统主义屠夫的藏身之所了？”

En: So his ideas tend to stir up trouble?
Zh: “这么说，他的思想总是会惹出些麻烦来？”

En: So... this must have been the hiding place of some naive leftists?
Zh: “所以……这里一定就是那些天真的左翼分子的藏身之处了？”

En: Why would someone have a portrait like this?
Zh: “世上怎么会有这种肖像？”

En: Yes, I can see that. Looks like some communists were hiding out here. They left a long time ago.
Zh: “我知道了，看来曾有些康米主义者在这里藏过身，不过他们已经离开很久了。”

En: The lieutenant does not seem to share your enthusiasm.
Zh: 警督对此似乎并没有像你一样的热情。

En: Yes. Looks like some latter-day radicals were hiding out here. They left a long time ago.
Zh: “是的。看起来某些近代的激进派在这里躲过，不过他们已经离开很久了。”

En: Some radical -- or radicals -- were hiding out here. They left a long time ago.
Zh: 他若有所思地点点头。“某位激进派——或某一群激进派——就在这里藏过身。他们很久以前就离开了。”

En: Brush the dust off the portrait.
Zh: 拂去肖像画上的灰尘。

En: Wonder: who is K. Mazov?
Zh: 心想：K·马佐夫是谁？

En: The man in the portrait looks back at you silently. He knows who he is, but he isn't telling.
Zh: 肖像画里的男人静静地回望着你。他知道自己是谁，但并不会告诉你。

En: Years' worth of dust is shaken off. The full head of hair, matched by an ample moustache and sideburns, look a bit silly.
Zh: 经年累月的积灰被拂去了。一头乱发，配上浓密的大胡子和鬓角，看起来有点傻。

En: Yes, some radical -- or radicals -- were hiding out here but left a long time ago.
Zh: 他若有所思地点点头。“某位激进派——或某一群激进派——在这里藏过身，不过他们已经离开很久了。”

En: Yes. And it looks like some latter-day radicals were hiding out here but left a long time ago.
Zh: “没错。某位激进派——或某一群激进派——在这里藏过身，不过他们已经离开很久了。”

En: If you wish. At any rate, looks like some Mazovians were hiding out here but left a long time ago.
Zh: “如果你愿意的话。话说回来，看起来某些马佐夫主义者在这里躲过，不过他们已经离开很久了。”

En: Looks like some radical -- or radicals -- were hiding out here but left a long time ago.
Zh: “某位激进派——或某一群激进派——在这里藏过身，不过他们已经离开很久了。”

En: The lieutenant does not seem to share your enthusiasm.
Zh: 警督对此似乎并没有像你一样的热情。

En: Someone crouches, heels digging into wet sand. Hands sweep across the sand, grains sticking to the frayed skin of the fingertips. An old man sits on a slab of concrete and taps his fingers against the glass of a scope... you shudder.
Zh: 有人蹲伏着，鞋跟深深陷入潮湿的沙子里。他的手拂过沙子，沙粒黏在指尖磨损的皮肤上。一个老人坐在一块混凝土板上，手指轻叩瞄准镜的玻璃……你不自主地打了个激灵。

En: Maybe I should move in here. Seems cozy.
Zh: “也许我应该搬到这里来住。好像还挺舒适的。”

En: What's with all these secret weapons caches, secret workshops, secret bunkers?
Zh: “这些秘密军火库，秘密工坊还有秘密碉堡是怎么回事？”

En: What's with all these secret workshops and secret bunkers?
Zh: “这些秘密工坊和秘密碉堡是怎么回事？”

En: What's with all these secret weapons caches and secret bunkers?
Zh: “这些秘密军火库和秘密碉堡是怎么回事？”

En: What's this still doing here?
Zh: “这玩意怎么还在这里？”

En: We have found a lot of those lately... I guess what most people think of as *history* tends to linger in run-down neighbourhoods. Martinaise being what it is, no one has gone through the trouble of cleaning out the old bunkers.
Zh: “咱们这几天已经发现不少这样的地方了。被大部分人称作*历史*的东西总会在破败的街区里阴魂不散很久。在马丁内斯这种地方，没人会愿意自找麻烦去把所有旧碉堡都清理一遍。”

En: What most people think of as *history* has a tendency to linger in run-down neighbourhoods. Martinaise being what it is, no one has gone through the trouble of cleaning out the old bunkers.
Zh: 他耸耸肩。“被大部分人称作*历史*的东西总会在破败的街区里阴魂不散很久。在马丁内斯这种地方，没人会愿意自找麻烦去把所有旧碉堡都清理一遍。”

En: I won't stand in your way -- but only after we're through with this case.
Zh: “我不会阻拦你——不过这要等到我们处理完这个案子之后。”
